Detailed Description

Represents a graph structure used for bucket-based optimization in a solver.

The BucketGraph class provides various functionalities for managing and optimizing a graph structure using buckets. It includes methods for initialization, configuration printing, node visitation checks, statistics printing, bucket assignment, and more.

The class also supports parallel arc generation, label management, and feasibility checks. It is designed to work with different directions (Forward and Backward) and stages of the optimization process.

Note
This class relies on several preprocessor directives (e.g., RIH, RCC, SRC) to conditionally enable or disable certain features.
Template Parameters
DirectionThe direction of the graph (Forward or Backward).
StageThe stage of the optimization process.
FullIndicates whether the full labeling algorithm should be used.
ArcTypeThe type of arc (Bucket or regular).
MutabilityIndicates whether the label is mutable or immutable.

BucketGraph::BucketGraph ( const std::vector< VRPNode > & nodes,
int horizon,
int bucket_interval,
int capacity,
int capacity_interval )

Constructs a BucketGraph object.

This constructor initializes a BucketGraph with the given nodes, time horizon, and bucket interval. It sets up the forward and backward buckets, initializes the dual values for the CVRP separation, and defines the intervals and resource limits.

◆ assign_buckets() [1/2]

template<Direction D>
auto & BucketGraph::assign_buckets ( auto & FW,
auto & BW )
inlineconstexprnoexcept

Assigns the buckets based on the specified direction.

This function returns a reference to the buckets based on the specified direction. If the direction is Forward, it returns a reference to the forward buckets. If the direction is Backward, it returns a reference to the backward buckets.

Template Parameters
DThe direction (Forward or Backward).
Parameters
FWThe forward buckets.
BWThe backward buckets.
Returns
A reference to the buckets based on the specified direction.

◆ augment_ng_memories()

void BucketGraph::augment_ng_memories ( std::vector< double > & solution,
std::vector< Path > & paths,
bool aggressive,
int eta1,
int eta2,
int eta_max,
int nC )

Augments the memories in the BucketGraph.

This function takes a solution vector, a SparseModel, and several parameters to augment the memories in the BucketGraph. It identifies cycles in the SparseModel that meet certain conditions and forbids them in the BucketGraph. The function prioritizes smaller cycles and limits the number of forbidden cycles based on the given parameters.

Definition at line 334 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

335 {
336 // Pre-allocate an estimated number of cycles.
337 std::vector<CycleData> cycles;
338 cycles.reserve(paths.size() / 4);
339 ng_iteration_counter++;
340
341 // Detect cycles in fractional paths.
342 for (size_t pathIndex = 0; pathIndex < paths.size(); ++pathIndex) {
343 const double epsilon = 1e-3;
344 double sol_val = solution[pathIndex];
345 if (sol_val <= epsilon) continue;
346 const auto &path = paths[pathIndex];
347 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<uint16_t, int> visited_clients;
348 visited_clients.reserve(path.size());
349
350 // Iterate over the nodes in the path to detect cycles.
351 for (size_t i = 0; i < path.size(); ++i) {
352 uint16_t client = path[i];
353 // Skip depot nodes.
354 if (client == 0 || client == N_SIZE - 1) continue;
355 auto it = visited_clients.find(client);
356 if (it != visited_clients.end()) {
357 // Cycle found: extract cycle from first occurrence to current
358 // index.
359 std::vector<uint16_t> cycle;
360 cycle.reserve(i - it->second + 1);
361 for (size_t j = it->second; j <= i; ++j) cycle.push_back(path[j]);
362
363 CycleData data;
364 data.cycle = std::move(cycle);
365 // Option 1: Use raw solution value as violation score.
366 // data.metric = sol_val;
367 // Option 2: Use fractionality (how far is sol_val from an
368 // integer).
369 data.metric = std::min(sol_val, 1.0 - sol_val);
370 cycles.push_back(std::move(data));
371 break; // Process only one cycle per path.
372 }
373 visited_clients[client] = i;
374 }
375 }
376 if (cycles.empty()) return;
377
378 // Sort cycles by descending metric value (i.e. cycles with highest
379 // violation or fractionality first).
380 pdqsort(cycles.begin(), cycles.end(), [](const CycleData &a, const CycleData &b) { return a.metric > b.metric; });
381
382 int forbidden_count = 0;
383
384 // Cache neighborhood bit counts to avoid redundant __builtin_popcountll
385 // calls.
386 std::vector<int> cached_counts(N_SIZE, -1);
387 auto count_neighborhood_bits = [this, eta_max, &cached_counts](uint16_t node) -> int {
388 if (cached_counts[node] >= 0) return cached_counts[node];
389 int count = 0;
390 for (const auto &segment : neighborhoods_bitmap[node]) {
391 count += __builtin_popcountll(segment);
392 if (count >= eta_max) break;
393 }
394 cached_counts[node] = count;
395 return count;
396 };
397
398 // Adapt thresholds based on the aggressive flag.
399 int effective_eta1 = eta1;
400 int effective_eta2 = eta2;
401
402 // Process each detected cycle.
403 for (const auto &data : cycles) {
404 const auto &cycle = data.cycle;
405 if (cycle.empty()) continue;
406
407 bool can_forbid = true;
408 // Check if any node in the cycle has too many neighbor bits.
409 for (uint16_t node : cycle) {
410 if (count_neighborhood_bits(node) >= eta_max) {
411 can_forbid = false;
412 break;
413 }
414 }
415
416 // If the cycle qualifies based on its size or our current count.
417 if (can_forbid && (static_cast<int>(cycle.size()) <= effective_eta1 || forbidden_count < effective_eta2)) {
418 std::vector<int> int_cycle(cycle.begin(), cycle.end());
419 forbidCycle(int_cycle, aggressive);
420 ++forbidden_count;
421 if (forbidden_count >= effective_eta2) break;
422 }
423 }
424 // If no cycle was added, try again without the effective_eta1 restriction.
425 if (forbidden_count == 0) {
426 for (const auto &data : cycles) {
427 const auto &cycle = data.cycle;
428 if (cycle.empty()) continue;
429
430 bool can_forbid = true;
431 for (uint16_t node : cycle) {
432 if (count_neighborhood_bits(node) >= eta_max) {
433 can_forbid = false;
434 break;
435 }
436 }
437 if (can_forbid && forbidden_count < effective_eta2) {
438 std::vector<int> int_cycle(cycle.begin(), cycle.end());
439 forbidCycle(int_cycle, aggressive);
440 ++forbidden_count;
441 if (forbidden_count >= effective_eta2) break;
442 }
443 }
444 }
445}

template<Stage S, Symmetry SYM>
std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::bi_labeling_algorithm ( )

Definition at line 560 of file BucketSolve.h.

560 {
561 // === Stage-specific Initializations ===
562 if constexpr (S == Stage::Three) {
563 heuristic_fixing<S>();
564 } else if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
565 if (first_reset) {
566 reset_fixed();
567 first_reset = false;
568 }
569#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
570 if (options.bucket_fixing) {
571 // heuristic_fixing<S>();
572
573 bucket_fixing<S>();
574 }
575#endif
576 // heuristic_fixing<S>();
577 }
578
579 if (options.warm_start && !just_fixed) {
580 capture_warm_start_labels<Direction::Forward>();
581 capture_warm_start_labels<Direction::Backward>();
582 }
583
584 // Reset pools and perform common initialization.
585 reset_pool();
586 common_initialization();
587
588 // Pre-allocate c-bar vectors for forward and backward buckets.
589 std::vector<double> forward_cbar(fw_buckets.size());
590 std::vector<double> backward_cbar(bw_buckets.size());
591
592 // Run the labeling algorithms.
593 if constexpr (SYM == Symmetry::Asymmetric) {
594 run_labeling_algorithms<S, Full::Partial>(forward_cbar, backward_cbar);
595 } else {
596 forward_cbar = labeling_algorithm<Direction::Forward, S, Full::Partial>();
597 }
598
599 // Acquire an initial best label from the forward label pool.
600 auto best_label = label_pool_fw->acquire();
601 if constexpr (SYM == Symmetry::Asymmetric) {
602 // Compute the best label if the forward and backward labels are
603 // feasible.
604 if (check_feasibility(fw_best_label, bw_best_label)) {
605 best_label = compute_label<S>(fw_best_label, bw_best_label);
606 } else {
607 best_label->cost = 0.0;
608 best_label->real_cost = std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
609 best_label->clearRoute();
610 }
611 } else {
612 best_label->cost = best_label->real_cost;
613 best_label->real_cost = std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
614 best_label->clearRoute();
615 }
616 merged_labels.push_back(best_label);
617
618 // Declare atomics to track the best cost and non-dominated label
619 // count.
620 std::atomic<double> best_cost{0.0};
621 std::atomic<size_t> non_dominated_labels_per_bucket{0};
622
623 // === Parallel Bucket Processing ===
624 // Choose a chunk size based on hardware concurrency.
625 const size_t chunk_size = fw_buckets_size / MERGE_SCHED_CONCURRENCY; // 1/4 of buckets per core
626 const size_t n_chunks = (fw_buckets_size + chunk_size - 1) / chunk_size;
627
628 auto bulk_sender = stdexec::bulk(
629 stdexec::just(), n_chunks,
630 [this, chunk_size, &best_cost, &non_dominated_labels_per_bucket](std::size_t chunk_idx) {
631 const size_t start_bucket = chunk_idx * chunk_size;
632 const size_t end_bucket = std::min(start_bucket + chunk_size, static_cast<size_t>(fw_buckets_size));
633
634 // Process each bucket within the current chunk.
635 for (size_t bucket = start_bucket; bucket < end_bucket; ++bucket) {
636 // Process non-dominated labels in the current bucket.
637 const auto &bucket_labels = fw_buckets[bucket].get_labels();
638 for (const Label *L : bucket_labels) {
639 if (L->is_dominated) continue;
640
641 non_dominated_labels_per_bucket.fetch_add(1, std::memory_order_relaxed);
642
643 // Retrieve the outgoing arcs for the current label.
644 // const auto &to_arcs =
645 // nodes[L->node_id].get_arcs<Direction::Forward>();
646 const auto &to_arcs = fw_buckets[bucket].template get_bucket_arcs<Direction::Forward>();
647 for (const auto &arc : to_arcs) {
648 const int to_node = fw_buckets[arc.to_bucket].node_id;
649 if constexpr (S >= Stage::Three || S == Stage::Eliminate) {
650 if (is_arc_fixed(L->node_id, to_node)) continue;
651 }
652
653 // Extend the label along the current arc.
654 auto L_prime =
655 Extend<Direction::Forward, S, ArcType::Bucket, Mutability::Const, Full::Reverse>(L, arc);
656
657 // Process each extended label.
658 if (L_prime != -1) {
659 int bucket_to_process = L_prime;
660
661 // Concatenate the label with bucket
662 // information, updating best_cost.
663 ConcatenateLabel<S, SYM>(L, bucket_to_process, best_cost);
664 }
665 }
666 }
667 }
668 });
669
670 // Submit the bulk work to the merge scheduler and wait for
671 // completion.
672 auto work = stdexec::starts_on(merge_sched, bulk_sender);
673 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(work));
674
675 // === Post-processing and Sorting ===
676 // Sort merged labels based on cost (ascending order).
677 pdqsort(merged_labels.begin(), merged_labels.end(),
678 [](const Label *a, const Label *b) { return a->cost < b->cost; });
679
680#ifdef SCHRODINGER
681 // If using the Schrödinger approach, process additional paths.
682 if (merged_labels.size() > N_ADD) {
683 std::vector<Path> paths;
684 const int labels_size = merged_labels.size();
685 const int end_idx = std::min(N_ADD + N_ADD, labels_size);
686 paths.reserve(end_idx - N_ADD);
687 for (int i = N_ADD; i < end_idx; ++i) {
688 const auto &route = merged_labels[i]->getRoute();
689 if (route.size() <= 3) continue;
690 paths.emplace_back(route, merged_labels[i]->real_cost);
691 }
692 sPool.add_paths(paths);
693 sPool.iterate();
694 }
695#endif
696
697#ifdef RIH
698 // If using RIH stage modifications, submit the top labels to ILS.
699 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
700 std::vector<Label *> top_labels;
701 top_labels.reserve(N_ADD);
702 const int n_candidates = std::min(N_ADD, static_cast<int>(merged_labels.size()));
703 for (int i = 0; i < n_candidates; ++i) {
704 if (merged_labels[i]->nodes_covered.size() <= 3) continue;
705 top_labels.push_back(merged_labels[i]);
706 }
707 ils->submit_task(top_labels, nodes);
708 }
709#endif
710
711 // Store the cost of the best (first) merged label as the inner
712 // objective.
713 inner_obj = merged_labels[0]->cost;
714 return merged_labels;
715}

◆ bucket_fixing()

template<Stage S>
void BucketGraph::bucket_fixing ( )

Fixes the bucket arcs for the specified stage.

This function performs the bucket arc fixing for the given stage. It initializes necessary variables and runs labeling algorithms to compute forward and backward reduced costs. Based on the computed gap, it performs arc elimination in both forward and backward directions and generates the necessary arcs.

Definition at line 936 of file BucketUtils.h.

936 {
937 // Only execute if not already fixed.
938 if (!fixed) {
939 fmt::print("\033[34m_STARTING BUCKET FIXING PROCEDURE\033[0m\n");
940 fixed = true;
941
942 // Compute gap and check feasibility (if gap is negative, exit
943 // early).
944 gap = std::ceil(incumbent - (relaxation + std::min(0.0, min_red_cost)));
945 if (gap < 0) {
946 fmt::print("\033[34m_BUCKET FIXING PROCEDURE CAN'T BE EXECUTED DUE TO "
947 "GAP\033[0m\n");
948 fmt::print("gap: {}\n", gap);
949 return;
950 }
951
952 // Initialize common structures, arc scores, warm labels, etc.
953 common_initialization();
954
955 // Initialize forward and backward c_bar vectors.
956 std::vector<double> forward_cbar(fw_buckets.size(), std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
957 std::vector<double> backward_cbar(bw_buckets.size(), std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
958
959 // Run labeling algorithms for Stage Four with full mode.
960 run_labeling_algorithms<Stage::Four, Full::Full>(forward_cbar, backward_cbar);
961 // Save computed c_bar values.
962 fw_c_bar = forward_cbar;
963 bw_c_bar = backward_cbar;
964
965 // Print info about bucket arc elimination.
966 print_info("performing bucket arc elimination with theta = {}\n", gap);
967
968 // Run forward and backward sections in parallel.
969 PARALLEL_SECTIONS(
970 work, bi_sched,
971 SECTION {
972 // Section 1: Process forward direction.
973 BucketArcElimination<Direction::Forward>(gap);
974 ObtainJumpBucketArcs<Direction::Forward>();
975 },
976 SECTION {
977 // Section 2: Process backward direction.
978 BucketArcElimination<Direction::Backward>(gap);
979 ObtainJumpBucketArcs<Direction::Backward>();
980 });
981
982 // Generate arcs after elimination.
983 // generate_arcs();
984 fmt::print("\033[34m_BUCKET FIXING PROCEDURE FINISHED\033[0m\n");
985
986 just_fixed = true;
987 return;
988 }
989 just_fixed = false;
990}

◆ BucketArcElimination()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::BucketArcElimination ( double theta)

Eliminates bucket arcs based on a given threshold.

This function performs bucket arc elimination in either the forward or backward direction, depending on the template parameter D. It resets all elements in the fixed buckets to 0, prints information about the direction of elimination, and processes arcs to update the local bucket arc map and fixed buckets.

Definition at line 181 of file BucketJump.h.

181 {
182 // Select direction-specific containers.
183 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
184 auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
185 auto &Phi_opposite = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_bw, Phi_fw);
186 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
187 auto &fixed_buckets_bitmap = assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets_bitmap, bw_fixed_buckets_bitmap);
188
189 // Reset fixed buckets bitmap.
190 fixed_buckets_bitmap.assign(fixed_buckets_bitmap.size(), 0);
191 const int n_buckets = buckets_size;
192 // Compute the number of 64-bit segments for bitmaps.
193 const size_t n_segments = (buckets_size + 63) / 64;
194
195 // Define ArcMap to store arc information.
196 using ArcMap = ankerl::unordered_dense::map<std::pair<std::pair<int, int>, int>, ankerl::unordered_dense::set<int>,
197 arc_map_hash>;
198 ArcMap local_B_Ba_b;
199 int removed_arcs = 0;
200
201 // Helper: create an arc key from two node IDs and a bucket.
202 auto create_arc_key = [](int from, int to, int b) { return std::make_pair(std::make_pair(from, to), b); };
203
204 // Helper lambda to quickly reset a bitmap vector.
205 auto reset_bitmap = [&](std::vector<uint64_t> &bitmap) {
206 // Using memset since our vector is contiguous.
207 if (!bitmap.empty()) std::memset(bitmap.data(), 0, bitmap.size() * sizeof(uint64_t));
208 };
209
210 // Check if there exists b' in BB such that b' <= bb_arr in the preorder
211 // induced by Phi_opposite (i.e., reachable from bb_arr through Phi*).
212 auto has_compatible_predecessor = [&](int bb_arr, const ankerl::unordered_dense::set<int> &BB) -> bool {
213 if (BB.find(bb_arr) != BB.end()) return true;
214
215 std::vector<int> stack;
216 stack.push_back(bb_arr);
217 std::vector<uint64_t> visited(n_segments, 0ULL);
218
219 while (!stack.empty()) {
220 const int curr = stack.back();
221 stack.pop_back();
222 const size_t seg = static_cast<size_t>(curr) >> 6;
223 const uint64_t mask = 1ULL << (curr & 63);
224 if (visited[seg] & mask) continue;
225 visited[seg] |= mask;
226
227 if (BB.find(curr) != BB.end()) return true;
228
229 for (int pred : Phi_opposite[curr]) {
230 const size_t pseg = static_cast<size_t>(pred) >> 6;
231 const uint64_t pmask = 1ULL << (pred & 63);
232 if ((visited[pseg] & pmask) == 0) { stack.push_back(pred); }
233 }
234 }
235 return false;
236 };
237
238 // Lambda: Process jump arcs for bucket 'b'.
239 auto process_jump_arcs = [&](int b) {
240 const auto &bucket_arcs = buckets[b].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
241 if (bucket_arcs.empty()) return;
242
243 std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited(n_segments, 0);
244 const auto &labels = buckets[b].get_labels();
245
246 for (const auto &a : bucket_arcs) {
247 if (!a.jump) continue;
248 std::vector<double> increment(options.resources.size(), 0.0);
249 for (size_t r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
250 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward)
251 increment[r] = buckets[b].lb[r] + a.resource_increment[r];
252 else
253 increment[r] = buckets[b].ub[r] - a.resource_increment[r];
254 }
255
256 auto arc_key = create_arc_key(buckets[a.from_bucket].node_id, buckets[a.to_bucket].node_id, b);
257 int b_opposite = get_opposite_bucket_number<D>(a.to_bucket, increment);
258 auto &Bidi_map = local_B_Ba_b[arc_key];
259
260 // Process each label in the current bucket.
261 for (auto &L_item : labels) {
262 // Reset Bvisited for each UpdateBucketsSet call.
263 reset_bitmap(Bvisited);
264 UpdateBucketsSet<D>(theta, L_item, Bidi_map, b_opposite, Bvisited);
265 }
266 }
267 };
268
269 // Lambda: Process bucket arcs for bucket 'b'.
270 auto process_bucket_arcs = [&](int b) {
271 const auto &bucket_arcs = buckets[b].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
272 if (bucket_arcs.empty()) return;
273
274 const auto &labels = buckets[b].get_labels();
275 std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited(n_segments, 0);
276
277 for (const auto &a : bucket_arcs) {
278 // Build the resource increment vector.
279 std::vector<double> increment(options.resources.size(), 0.0);
280 for (size_t r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
281 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward)
282 increment[r] = buckets[b].lb[r] + a.resource_increment[r];
283 else
284 increment[r] = buckets[b].ub[r] - a.resource_increment[r];
285 }
286
287 auto arc_key = create_arc_key(buckets[a.from_bucket].node_id, buckets[a.to_bucket].node_id, b);
288 int b_opposite = get_opposite_bucket_number<D>(a.to_bucket, increment);
289 auto &Bidi_map = local_B_Ba_b[arc_key];
290
291 // Process each label in bucket 'b'.
292 for (auto &L_item : labels) {
293 reset_bitmap(Bvisited);
294 UpdateBucketsSet<D>(theta, L_item, Bidi_map, b_opposite, Bvisited);
295 }
296
297 // Fix gamma if there is no compatible predecessor bucket in BB.
298 if (!has_compatible_predecessor(b_opposite, Bidi_map)) {
299 const size_t bit_pos = static_cast<size_t>(a.from_bucket) * n_buckets + a.to_bucket;
300 fixed_buckets_bitmap[bit_pos / 64] |= (1ULL << (bit_pos % 64));
301 ++removed_arcs;
302 }
303 }
304 };
305
306 // Process each bucket.
307 for (int b = 0; b < buckets_size; ++b) {
308 // Process node arcs.
309 const auto &node_arcs = nodes[buckets[b].node_id].template get_arcs<D>();
310 for (const auto &a : node_arcs) {
311 auto arc_key = std::make_pair(std::make_pair(a.from, a.to), b);
312 auto &Bidi_map = local_B_Ba_b[arc_key];
313
314 if (!Phi[b].empty()) {
315 // Merge neighbor bucket arcs.
316 for (const auto &b_prime : Phi[b]) {
317 auto neighbor_key = std::make_pair(std::make_pair(a.from, a.to), b_prime);
318 if (auto it = local_B_Ba_b.find(neighbor_key); it != local_B_Ba_b.end()) {
319 Bidi_map.insert(it->second.begin(), it->second.end());
320 }
321 }
322 }
323 }
324
325 // Process jump arcs and bucket arcs.
326 process_jump_arcs(b);
327 process_bucket_arcs(b);
328
329 // iterate over fixed buckets and remove them
330 for (int b_prime = 0; b_prime < buckets_size; ++b_prime) {
331 if (is_bucket_fixed<D>(b, b_prime)) { buckets[b].template remove_bucket_arc<D>(b, b_prime); }
332 }
333 }
334
335 // Print the total number of removed arcs.
336 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward)
337 print_info("[Fw] removed arcs: {}\n", removed_arcs);
338 else
339 print_info("[Bw] removed arcs: {}\n", removed_arcs);
340}

◆ calculate_neighborhoods()

void BucketGraph::calculate_neighborhoods ( size_t num_closest)

Calculates the neighborhoods for each node for the ng-routes.

Definition at line 234 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

234 {
235 const size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
236
237 // Resize the neighborhoods_bitmap vector so that there's one bitmap per
238 // node.
239 neighborhoods_bitmap.resize(num_nodes);
240
241 // Pre-calculate the number of segments needed (each segment is 64 bits).
242 const size_t num_segments = (num_nodes + 63) / 64;
243
244 // Process each node to compute its neighborhood.
245 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
246 // Create a vector of (distance, node_index) pairs for node i.
247 std::vector<std::pair<double, size_t>> forward_distances;
248 forward_distances.reserve(num_nodes - 1);
249
250 for (size_t j = 0; j < num_nodes; ++j) {
251 if (i == j) continue;
252 double distance = getcij(i, j);
253 forward_distances.push_back({distance, j});
254 }
255
256 // Sort distances to obtain the closest nodes first.
257 pdqsort(forward_distances.begin(), forward_distances.end(),
258 [](const auto &a, const auto &b) { return a.first < b.first; });
259
260 // Initialize the neighborhood bitmap for node i with all segments set
261 // to zero.
262 neighborhoods_bitmap[i].assign(num_segments, 0ULL);
263
264 // Always include the node itself in its neighborhood.
265 size_t self_segment = i >> 6;
266 size_t self_bit = i & 63;
267 neighborhoods_bitmap[i][self_segment] |= (1ULL << self_bit);
268
269 // Add the top 'num_closest' closest nodes as forward neighbors.
270 for (size_t k = 0; k < num_closest && k < forward_distances.size(); ++k) {
271 size_t neighbor_index = forward_distances[k].second;
272 size_t segment = neighbor_index >> 6;
273 size_t bit_position = neighbor_index & 63;
274 neighborhoods_bitmap[i][segment] |= (1ULL << bit_position);
275 }
276 }
277}

502 {
503 auto &warm_labels = assign_buckets<D>(fw_warm_labels, bw_warm_labels);
504 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
505 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
506
507 warm_labels.clear();
508 if (!options.warm_start || just_fixed) return;
509
510 warm_labels.reserve(buckets_size);
511 for (int bucket = 0; bucket < buckets_size; ++bucket) {
512 const Label *label = buckets[bucket].get_best_label();
513 if (!label) continue;
514
515 WarmLabelState snapshot;
516 snapshot.cost = label->cost;
517 snapshot.node_id = label->node_id;
518 snapshot.resources = label->resources;
519 snapshot.nodes_covered = label->nodes_covered;
520 snapshot.path_len = label->path_len;
521 warm_labels.push_back(std::move(snapshot));
522 }

◆ check_feasibility()

bool BucketGraph::check_feasibility ( const Label * fw_label,
const Label * bw_label )
inline

Checks the feasibility of a given forward and backward label.

This function determines if the transition from a forward label to a backward label is feasible based on resource constraints and node durations.

Definition at line 808 of file BucketGraph.h.

808 {
809 if (!fw_label || !bw_label) return false;
810
811 // Cache resources and node data.
812 const auto &fw_resources = fw_label->resources;
813 const auto &bw_resources = bw_label->resources;
814 const auto &node = nodes[fw_label->node_id];
815
816 const size_t n_resources = options.resources.size();
817
818 // Compute travel time only once.
819 const auto travel_time = getcij(fw_label->node_id, bw_label->node_id);
820
821 // Assuming the "time" resource is at a known index (e.g., index 0).
822 // If not, you can either iterate to find it once or precompute a
823 // boolean array.
824 if (n_resources > 0 && options.resources[0] == "time") {
825 // Check time feasibility.
826 if (numericutils::gt(fw_resources[0] + travel_time + node.duration, bw_resources[0])) return false;
827 } else {
828 // Fallback: iterate to check any resource named "time".
829 for (size_t r = 0; r < n_resources; ++r) {
830 if (options.resources[r] == "time") {
831 if (numericutils::gt(fw_resources[r] + travel_time + node.duration, bw_resources[r])) return false;
832 }
833 }
834 }
835
836 // Check additional resource feasibility (assuming resources[0] is
837 // "time") and additional resources (from index 1 onward) represent
838 // capacity constraints.
839 for (size_t i = 1; i < n_resources; ++i) {
840 if (fw_resources[i] + node.demand > bw_resources[i]) return false;
841 }
842
843 return true;
844 }

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::common_initialization ( )

Initializes the BucketGraph by clearing previous data and setting up forward and backward buckets.

Definition at line 1286 of file BucketUtils.h.

1286 {
1287 // --- Pre-allocate and clear temporary vectors ---
1288 merged_labels.clear();
1289 merged_labels.reserve(50);
1290
1291 // Retrieve active cuts and build a vector of ActiveCutInfo.
1292 auto &cutter = cut_storage;
1293 const auto active_cuts = cutter->getActiveCuts();
1294 std::vector<ActiveCutInfo> active_cuts_info;
1295 active_cuts_info.reserve(active_cuts.size());
1296 for (const auto &cut : active_cuts) { active_cuts_info.push_back(cut); }
1297
1298 // --- Sort arcs by scores for each node ---
1299 auto &arc_scores = assign_buckets<D>(fw_arc_scores, bw_arc_scores);
1300 for (auto &node : nodes) {
1301 if (!arc_scores[node.id].empty()) { node.sort_arcs_by_scores<D>(arc_scores[node.id], nodes, active_cuts_info); }
1302 arc_scores[node.id].clear();
1303 }
1304
1305 auto &warm_labels = assign_buckets<D>(fw_warm_labels, bw_warm_labels);
1306 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
1307 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
1308 auto &c_bar = assign_buckets<D>(fw_c_bar, bw_c_bar);
1309 auto &bucket_splits = assign_buckets<D>(fw_bucket_splits, bw_bucket_splits);
1310
1311 // --- Initialize c_bar vector ---
1312 c_bar.resize(buckets_size, std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
1313
1314 // --- Initialize dominance check counters for forward direction ---
1315 if constexpr (Direction::Forward == D) {
1316 dominance_checks_per_bucket.assign(buckets_size + 1, 0);
1317 non_dominated_labels_per_bucket = 0;
1318 }
1319
1320 // --- Clear all buckets ---
1321 for (size_t b = 0; b < buckets_size; b++) { buckets[b].clear(); }
1322
1323 // --- Warm start processing: sort warm_labels and compute reduced costs
1324 // ---
1325 if (options.warm_start && !just_fixed && !warm_labels.empty()) {
1326 pdqsort(warm_labels.begin(), warm_labels.end(),
1327 [](const WarmLabelState &a, const WarmLabelState &b) { return a.cost < b.cost; });
1328 const size_t process_size =
1329 std::min(warm_labels.size(), static_cast<size_t>(options.n_warm_start * warm_labels.size()));
1330 for (size_t i = 0; i < process_size; ++i) {
1331 auto new_label = compute_red_cost(warm_labels[i], D == Direction::Forward);
1332 if (new_label != nullptr) {
1333 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
1334 fw_buckets[new_label->vertex].add_sorted_label(new_label);
1335 } else {
1336 bw_buckets[new_label->vertex].add_sorted_label(new_label);
1337 }
1338 }
1339 }
1340 warm_labels.clear();
1341 }
1342
1343 // --- Prepare interval arrays and compute per-resource splits for depot
1344 // ---
1345 const size_t num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
1346 std::vector<double> base_intervals(num_intervals);
1347 std::vector<double> interval_starts(num_intervals);
1348 std::vector<double> interval_ends(num_intervals);
1349 // Compute per-resource split counts for the depot
1350 const auto &VRPNode = nodes[0]; // assuming node 0 is the depot
1351 std::vector<int> depot_splits(num_intervals);
1352 for (size_t r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
1353 // Use the same ratio as earlier:
1354 double full_range = R_max[r] - R_min[r];
1355 double node_range = VRPNode.ub[r] - VRPNode.lb[r];
1356 depot_splits[r] = std::max(1, static_cast<int>(std::round((node_range / full_range) * intervals[r].interval)));
1357 // Compute the base interval for resource r.
1358 base_intervals[r] = (VRPNode.ub[r] - VRPNode.lb[r]) / depot_splits[r];
1359 interval_starts[r] = VRPNode.lb[r];
1360 interval_ends[r] = VRPNode.ub[r];
1361 }
1362
1363 // --- Interval-based bucket generation ---
1364 std::vector<int> current_pos(num_intervals, 0);
1365 int offset = 0;
1366 // Lambda for generating interval combinations and creating depots.
1367 auto generate_combinations = [&](bool is_forward, int &offset) {
1368 auto &label_pool = is_forward ? label_pool_fw : label_pool_bw;
1369 auto &buckets = is_forward ? fw_buckets : bw_buckets;
1370 const auto depot_id = is_forward ? options.depot : options.end_depot;
1371 const int calculated_index_base =
1372 is_forward ? num_buckets_index_fw[options.depot] : num_buckets_index_bw[options.end_depot];
1373
1374 std::vector<double> interval_bounds(num_intervals);
1375 // Recursive lambda to generate all interval combinations.
1376 std::function<void(int)> process_intervals = [&](int depth) {
1377 if (depth == num_intervals) {
1378 // Acquire a new depot label.
1379 auto depot = label_pool->acquire();
1380 int calculated_index = calculated_index_base + offset;
1381 for (int r = 0; r < static_cast<int>(num_intervals); ++r) {
1382 // For forward direction, start at the lower bound and
1383 // add multiples of the base interval; adjust similarly
1384 // for backward.
1385 interval_bounds[r] = is_forward ? interval_starts[r] + current_pos[r] * base_intervals[r]
1386 : interval_ends[r] - current_pos[r] * base_intervals[r];
1387 }
1388 depot->initialize(calculated_index, 0.0, interval_bounds, depot_id);
1389 depot->is_extended = false;
1390 depot->addNode(depot_id);
1391 set_node_visited(depot->visited_bitmap, depot_id);
1392 SRC_MODE_BLOCK(depot->SRCmap.assign(cut_storage->SRCDuals.size(), 0);)
1393 buckets[calculated_index].add_label(depot);
1394 buckets[calculated_index].node_id = depot_id;
1395
1396 offset++;
1397 return;
1398 }
1399
1400 // For the current resource dimension, loop using its specific
1401 // split count.
1402 for (int k = 0; k < depot_splits[depth]; ++k) {
1403 current_pos[depth] = k;
1404 process_intervals(depth + 1);
1405 }
1406 };
1407 process_intervals(0);
1408 };
1409
1410 // Generate combinations for the current direction.
1411 generate_combinations(D == Direction::Forward, offset);
1412}
SRC_MODE_BLOCK

template<Stage S>
Label * BucketGraph::compute_label ( const Label * L,

Definition at line 1274 of file BucketSolve.h.

1274 {
1275 // Compute cost values
1276 double cij_cost = getcij(L->node_id, L_prime->node_id);
1277 double real_cost = L->real_cost + L_prime->real_cost + cij_cost;
1278 const auto &forward_route = L->nodes_covered;
1279 const auto &backward_route = L_prime->nodes_covered;
1280
1281 // Acquire a new label from the pool and initialize its cost fields.
1282 auto new_label = label_pool_fw->acquire();
1283 new_label->cost = red_cost;
1284 new_label->real_cost = real_cost;
1285 new_label->parent = nullptr;
1286 new_label->path_len = L->path_len + L_prime->path_len;
1287
1288 // Reuse pooled vector capacity and avoid zero-filling before overwrite.
1289 auto &merged_route = new_label->nodes_covered;
1290 merged_route.clear();
1291 merged_route.reserve(forward_route.size() + backward_route.size());
1292 merged_route.insert(merged_route.end(), forward_route.begin(), forward_route.end());
1293 merged_route.insert(merged_route.end(), backward_route.rbegin(), backward_route.rend());
1294
1295 return new_label;
1296}

Label * BucketGraph::compute_mono_label ( const Label * L)

Computes the mono label for the BucketGraph.

This function computes the mono label for the BucketGraph by acquiring a new label from the label pool and setting the cost and real cost values from the given label L. It then calculates the number of nodes covered by the label and its ancestors, reserves space for the nodes covered, and inserts the nodes from the

◆ compute_red_cost()

Label * BucketGraph::compute_red_cost ( const WarmLabelState & L,
bool fw )
inline

Definition at line 1097 of file BucketGraph.h.

1097 {
1098 // Calculate main and reduced costs.
1099 double real_cost = 0.0;
1100 double red_cost = 0.0;
1101
1102 // Check main resource feasibility.
1103 const double main_resource = L.resources[options.main_resources[0]];
1104 const double q_star_value = q_star[options.main_resources[0]];
1105 if (fw) {
1106 if (main_resource > q_star_value) return nullptr;
1107 } else {
1108 if (main_resource <= q_star_value) return nullptr;
1109 }
1110
1111 // Ensure sufficient nodes in the route.
1112 if (L.path_len <= 3) return nullptr;
1113
1114 // Initialize an empty SRCmap vector with size equal to the current
1115 // number of cuts.
1116 std::vector<uint16_t> updated_SRCmap(cut_storage->size(), 0);
1117
1118 // Prepare new label.
1119 auto pool = fw ? label_pool_fw : label_pool_bw;
1120 auto new_label = pool->acquire();
1121 auto &route = new_label->nodes_covered;
1122 route.clear();
1123 route.reserve(L.nodes_covered.size());
1124 route.insert(route.end(), L.nodes_covered.begin(), L.nodes_covered.end());
1125 new_label->path_len = L.path_len;
1126
1127 // Variables to keep track of costs.
1128 int last_node = -1;
1129
1130 // Traverse the nodes in the route (from current label to root).
1131 for (auto node_id : L.nodes_covered) {
1132 // --- NG-route Feasibility Check ---
1133 // Clear visited bitmap in the neighborhood of node_id.
1134 for (size_t seg = 0, limit = new_label->visited_bitmap.size(); seg < limit; ++seg) {
1135 uint64_t current_visited = new_label->visited_bitmap[seg];
1136 if (!current_visited) continue;
1137 uint64_t neighborhood_mask = neighborhoods_bitmap[node_id][seg];
1138 new_label->visited_bitmap[seg] = current_visited & neighborhood_mask;
1139 }
1140 set_node_visited(new_label->visited_bitmap, node_id);
1141
1142 // --- Cost Accumulation ---
1143 if (last_node != -1) {
1144 double cij_cost = getcij(last_node, node_id);
1145 real_cost += cij_cost;
1146 red_cost += cij_cost;
1147 }
1148 red_cost -= nodes[node_id].cost;
1149
1150 // --- SRC Logic ---
1151 size_t segment = node_id >> 6;
1152 const uint64_t bit_mask = bit_mask_lookup[node_id & 63];
1153 auto &cutter = cut_storage;
1154 auto &SRCDuals = cutter->SRCDuals;
1155
1156#if defined(SRC)
1157 for (std::size_t idx = 0; idx < cutter->size(); ++idx) {
1158 const auto &cut = cutter->getCut(idx);
1159 // Retrieve base set and order information.
1160 const auto &baseSet = cut.baseSet;
1161 const auto &baseSetOrder = cut.baseSetOrder;
1162 const auto &neighbors = cut.neighbors;
1163 const auto &multipliers = cut.p;
1164
1165 // If the current node is not in the neighbor set, reset SRCmap
1166 // value.
1167 if (!(neighbors[segment] & bit_mask)) {
1168 updated_SRCmap[idx] = 0;
1169 continue;
1170 }
1171 // If the current node is in the base set, update SRC value.
1172 if (baseSet[segment] & bit_mask) {
1173 auto &src_val = updated_SRCmap[idx];
1174 src_val += multipliers.num[baseSetOrder[node_id]];
1175 if (src_val >= multipliers.den) {
1176 red_cost -= SRCDuals[idx];
1177 src_val -= multipliers.den;
1178 }
1179 }
1180 }
1181#endif
1182
1183 // --- Arc and Branching Dual Adjustments ---
1184#if defined(RCC) || defined(EXACT_RCC)
1185 if (last_node != -1) { red_cost -= arc_duals.getDual(last_node, node_id); }
1186#endif
1187 if (branching_duals->size() > 0 && last_node != -1) {
1188 red_cost -= branching_duals->getDual(last_node, node_id);
1189 }
1190
1191 last_node = node_id; // Update last_node.
1192 }
1193
1194 // Set final label properties.
1195 new_label->cost = red_cost;
1196 new_label->real_cost = real_cost;
1197 new_label->parent = nullptr;
1198 new_label->node_id = L.node_id;
1199 new_label->is_extended = false;
1200 new_label->resources = L.resources;
1201 new_label->SRCmap = updated_SRCmap;
1202 new_label->fresh = false;
1203
1204 // Determine bucket number for the new label.
1205 std::vector<double> new_resources(options.resources.size());
1206 for (size_t i = 0; i < options.resources.size(); ++i) { new_resources[i] = new_label->resources[i]; }
1207 int bucket = fw ? get_bucket_number<Direction::Forward>(new_label->node_id, new_resources)
1208 : get_bucket_number<Direction::Backward>(new_label->node_id, new_resources);
1209 new_label->vertex = bucket;
1210
1211 return new_label;
1212 }

◆ computePhi()

std::vector< int > BucketGraph::computePhi ( int & bucket_id,
bool fw )

Computes the phi values for a given bucket ID and direction.

Definition at line 146 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

146 {
147 std::vector<int> phi;
148
149 // Get references based on the direction.
150 auto &buckets = fw ? fw_buckets : bw_buckets;
151
152 // Multi-dimensional resource case.
153 if (options.resources.size() > 1) {
154 if (bucket_id < 0 || bucket_id >= static_cast<int>(buckets.size())) return phi;
155
156 const int n_dims = static_cast<int>(intervals.size());
157 std::vector<double> base_intervals(n_dims, 0.0);
158
159 // Get the node associated with the current bucket.
160 int node_id = buckets[bucket_id].node_id;
161 const VRPNode &node = nodes[node_id];
162
163 // Recompute splits and base intervals per dimension using the same
164 // logic as in define_buckets.
165 for (int r = 0; r < n_dims; ++r) {
166 double full_range = R_max[r] - R_min[r];
167 double node_range = node.ub[r] - node.lb[r];
168 int splits = 1;
169 if (std::fabs(full_range) > std::numeric_limits<double>::epsilon()) {
170 double splits_d = (node_range * intervals[r].interval) / full_range;
171 splits = std::max(1, static_cast<int>(std::round(splits_d)));
172 }
173 base_intervals[r] = node_range / static_cast<double>(splits);
174 }
175
176 const auto &current_bucket = buckets[bucket_id];
177 std::vector<double> target(n_dims, 0.0);
178
179 // Use direction-consistent anchors:
180 // - Forward buckets are indexed low→high, so predecessor is one step
181 // down from lb.
182 // - Backward buckets are indexed high→low, so predecessor is one step
183 // up from ub.
184 for (int r = 0; r < n_dims; ++r) { target[r] = fw ? current_bucket.lb[r] : current_bucket.ub[r]; }
185
186 // Adjust the target vector by one base interval for each dimension.
187 // Forward predecessor: lower-resource bucket.
188 // Backward predecessor (high→low indexing): higher-resource bucket.
189 for (int r = 0; r < n_dims; ++r) {
190 if (fw)
191 target[r] = std::max(node.lb[r], target[r] - base_intervals[r]);
192 else
193 target[r] = std::min(node.ub[r], target[r] + base_intervals[r]);
194 }
195
196 // Closed-form lookup consistent with direction-specific bucket
197 // indexing.
198 int candidate_bucket = fw ? get_bucket_number<Direction::Forward>(node_id, target)
199 : get_bucket_number<Direction::Backward>(node_id, target);
200 if (candidate_bucket >= 0 && candidate_bucket < static_cast<int>(buckets.size()) &&
201 candidate_bucket != bucket_id && buckets[candidate_bucket].node_id == node_id) {
202#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
203 bool not_fixed = fw ? is_bucket_not_fixed_forward(bucket_id, candidate_bucket)
204 : is_bucket_not_fixed_backward(bucket_id, candidate_bucket);
205 // if (not_fixed)
206#endif
207 { phi.push_back(candidate_bucket); }
208 }
209 }
210 // Single-resource case (simpler).
211 else {
212 // Forward: predecessor = lower-resource bucket (bucket_id - 1).
213 // Backward (high→low indexing): predecessor is also one index lower,
214 // because lower index means higher resource.
215 int neighbor = bucket_id - 1;
216 bool valid_neighbor = (neighbor >= 0);
217 if (valid_neighbor && neighbor >= 0 && neighbor < static_cast<int>(buckets.size()) &&
218 buckets[neighbor].node_id == buckets[bucket_id].node_id) {
219#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
220 bool not_fixed = fw ? is_bucket_not_fixed_forward(neighbor, bucket_id)
221 : is_bucket_not_fixed_backward(neighbor, bucket_id);
222 // if (not_fixed)
223#endif
224 { phi.push_back(neighbor); }
225 }
226 }
227 return phi;
228}

◆ ConcatenateLabel()

template<Stage S, Symmetry SYM>
void BucketGraph::ConcatenateLabel ( const Label * L,
int & b,
std::atomic< double > & best_cost )

Concatenates the label L with the bucket b and updates the best label pbest.

Definition at line 586 of file BucketUtils.h.

586 {
587 // Determine the number of segments needed for the bucket visited
588 // bitmap.
589 const size_t n_segments = (fw_buckets_size + 63) / 64;
590 static thread_local std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited;
591 if (Bvisited.size() != n_segments) { Bvisited.resize(n_segments); }
592 std::memset(Bvisited.data(), 0, n_segments * sizeof(uint64_t));
593
594 // Use thread_local storage for the bucket stack to avoid reallocations.
595 static thread_local std::vector<int> bucket_stack;
596 if (bucket_stack.capacity() < 64) bucket_stack.reserve(64);
597 bucket_stack.clear(); // Clear for reuse
598 bucket_stack.push_back(b);
599
600 static thread_local std::vector<std::pair<const Label *, double>> merge_candidates;
601
602 // Choose symmetric buckets and cost-bar arrays based on symmetry.
603 auto &other_buckets = assign_symmetry<SYM>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
604 auto &other_c_bar = assign_symmetry<SYM>(fw_c_bar, bw_c_bar);
605
606 // Cache frequently accessed values.
607 const int L_node_id = L->node_id;
608 const double L_cost = L->cost;
609
610 // Pre-compute if branching is active.
611 const bool has_branching = !branching_duals->empty();
612
613#if defined(SRC)
614 // SRC mode setup: Only relevant in Stage::Four.
615 decltype(cut_storage) cutter = nullptr;
616 if constexpr (S > Stage::Three) { cutter = cut_storage; }
617 const auto active_cuts = cutter->getActiveCuts();
618#endif
619
620// Lambda to apply SRC adjustments to total_cost (only for Stage::Four).
621#if defined(SRC)
622 auto applySRCAdjustments = [&](double total_cost, const Label *L, const Label *L_bw) -> double {
623 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
624 for (const auto &active_cut : active_cuts) {
625 const auto &cut = *active_cut.cut_ptr;
626 const size_t idx = active_cut.index;
627 const double dual = active_cut.dual_value;
628 if (L->SRCmap[idx] + L_bw->SRCmap[idx] >= cut.p.den) { total_cost -= dual; }
629 }
630 }
631 return total_cost;
632 };
633#endif
634
635 // Process buckets until the stack is empty.
636 while (!bucket_stack.empty()) {
637 const int current_bucket = bucket_stack.back();
638 bucket_stack.pop_back();
639
640 // Mark current_bucket as visited in the bitmap.
641 const size_t segment = current_bucket >> 6;
642 const uint64_t bit_mask = bit_mask_lookup[current_bucket & 63];
643 Bvisited[segment] |= bit_mask;
644
645 // Get the node id associated with this bucket.
646 const int bucketLprimenode = other_buckets[current_bucket].node_id;
647 double travel_cost = getcij(L_node_id, bucketLprimenode);
648
649#if defined(RCC) || defined(EXACT_RCC)
650 // Adjust travel cost with arc duals in Stage Four.
651 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) { travel_cost -= arc_duals.getDual(L_node_id, bucketLprimenode); }
652#endif
653
654 if (has_branching) { travel_cost -= branching_duals->getDual(L_node_id, bucketLprimenode); }
655
656 const double path_cost = L_cost + travel_cost;
657 const double bound = other_c_bar[current_bucket];
658
659 const double prune_limit = best_cost.load(std::memory_order_relaxed);
660
661 // Early bound check.
662 if ((S != Stage::Enumerate && numericutils::gte(path_cost + bound, prune_limit)) ||
663 (S == Stage::Enumerate && numericutils::gte(path_cost + bound, gap))) {
664 continue;
665 }
666
667 // Retrieve labels in the current bucket.
668 const auto &bucket = other_buckets[current_bucket];
669 const auto &labels = bucket.get_labels();
670 if (labels.empty()) continue;
671 merge_candidates.clear();
672 if (merge_candidates.capacity() < labels.size()) { merge_candidates.reserve(labels.size()); }
673
674 // Process each label in the current bucket.
675 for (const Label *L_bw : labels) {
676 // Skip invalid labels with combined check
677 if (L_bw == nullptr || L_bw->is_dominated || L_bw->node_id == L_node_id) { continue; }
678
679 // Check feasibility
680 if (!check_feasibility(L, L_bw)) { continue; }
681
682 double total_cost = path_cost + L_bw->cost;
683
684#if defined(SRC)
685 // Apply SRC adjustments if in Stage Four.
686 total_cost = applySRCAdjustments(total_cost, L, L_bw);
687#endif
688 double latest_best = best_cost.load(std::memory_order_relaxed);
689
690 // Filter based on total cost against best known cost or gap.
691 bool cost_acceptable;
692 if constexpr (S != Stage::Enumerate) {
693 cost_acceptable = numericutils::lt(total_cost, latest_best);
694 } else {
695 cost_acceptable = numericutils::lt(total_cost, gap);
696 }
697
698 if (!cost_acceptable) { continue; }
699 merge_candidates.emplace_back(L_bw, total_cost);
700 }
701
702 if (!merge_candidates.empty()) {
703 // Replay accepted candidates in order under one lock.
704 std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(merge_mutex);
705
706 double current_best = best_cost.load(std::memory_order_relaxed);
707 for (const auto &[candidate_label, candidate_cost] : merge_candidates) {
708 if (numericutils::lt(candidate_cost, current_best)) {
709 best_cost.store(candidate_cost, std::memory_order_relaxed);
710 current_best = candidate_cost;
711 }
712
713 if (numericutils::lte(candidate_cost, current_best)) {
714 auto pbest = compute_label<S>(L, candidate_label, candidate_cost);
715 merged_labels.push_back(pbest);
716 }
717 }
718 }
719 // Add neighbor buckets to the stack if they have not been visited.
720 const auto &neighbors = Phi_bw[current_bucket];
721 if (bucket_stack.capacity() < bucket_stack.size() + neighbors.size()) {
722 bucket_stack.reserve(bucket_stack.size() + neighbors.size());
723 }
724 for (int b_prime : neighbors) {
725 const size_t seg_prime = b_prime >> 6;
726 const uint64_t mask_prime = bit_mask_lookup[b_prime & 63];
727 if (!(Bvisited[seg_prime] & mask_prime)) { bucket_stack.push_back(b_prime); }
728 }
729 }
730}

◆ define_buckets() [1/2]

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::define_buckets ( )

Defines the buckets for the BucketGraph.

This function determines the number of buckets based on the time intervals and assigns buckets to the graph. It computes resource bounds for each vertex and defines the bounds of each bucket.

Definition at line 160 of file BucketUtils.h.

160 {
161 // 1. Save the old buckets and fixed status.
162 std::vector<Bucket> old_buckets;
163 std::vector<bool> was_fixed;
164 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
165 size_t old_size = buckets.size();
166
167 // Map to store the fixed range (min_lb and max_ub) for each node pair.
168 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<int,
169 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<int, std::pair<std::vector<double>, std::vector<double>>>>
170 node_pair_fixed_ranges;
171
172 if (old_size > 0) {
173 old_buckets = buckets;
174 was_fixed.resize(old_size * old_size, false);
175 auto &old_bitmap = assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets_bitmap, bw_fixed_buckets_bitmap);
176
177 // Mark fixed bucket pairs.
178 for (size_t i = 0; i < old_size; ++i) {
179 for (size_t j = 0; j < old_size; ++j) {
180 size_t pos = i * old_size + j;
181 if (pos / 64 < old_bitmap.size()) {
182 was_fixed[pos] = ((old_bitmap[pos / 64] & (1ULL << (pos % 64))) != 0);
183 }
184 }
185 }
186
187 const int num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
188 // For each fixed bucket pair, update the fixed range for the
189 // corresponding node pair.
190 for (size_t i = 0; i < old_size; ++i) {
191 for (size_t j = 0; j < old_size; ++j) {
192 if (!was_fixed[i * old_size + j]) continue;
193 const auto &bucket_from = old_buckets[i];
194 const auto &bucket_to = old_buckets[j];
195 int from_node = bucket_from.node_id;
196 int to_node = bucket_to.node_id;
197 // Initialize if necessary.
198 if (node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node].find(to_node) == node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node].end()) {
199 node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node][to_node] = {
200 std::vector<double>(num_intervals, std::numeric_limits<double>::max()),
201 std::vector<double>(num_intervals, std::numeric_limits<double>::lowest())};
202 }
203 auto &[min_lbs, max_ubs] = node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node][to_node];
204 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
205 min_lbs[r] = std::min(min_lbs[r], bucket_from.lb[r]);
206 max_ubs[r] = std::max(max_ubs[r], bucket_from.ub[r]);
207 }
208 }
209 }
210 } // End if old buckets exist.
211
212 // 2. Clear the current buckets and fixed bitmap.
213 buckets.clear();
214 auto &fixed_buckets_bitmap = assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets_bitmap, bw_fixed_buckets_bitmap);
215 fixed_buckets_bitmap.clear();
216
217 // 3. Prepare variables and containers for new bucket definition.
218 const int num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
219 std::vector<double> total_ranges(num_intervals); // unused in current code
220 std::vector<double> base_intervals(num_intervals); // unused in current code
221
222 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
223 auto &num_buckets_index = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
224 auto &node_interval_trees = assign_buckets<D>(fw_node_interval_trees, bw_node_interval_trees);
225 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
226 auto &bucket_splits = assign_buckets<D>(fw_bucket_splits, bw_bucket_splits);
227
228 const size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
229 num_buckets.resize(num_nodes);
230 num_buckets_index.resize(num_nodes);
231 node_interval_trees.assign(num_nodes, SplayTree());
232
233 // Lambda to calculate an interval for one dimension.
234 auto calculate_interval = [&](double lb, double ub, double base_interval, int pos, int max_intervals,
235 bool is_forward) -> std::pair<double, double> {
236 double start, end;
237 if (is_forward) {
238 // For the first interval, start exactly at lb.
239 start = (pos == 0) ? lb : lb + pos * base_interval;
240 // For the last interval, end exactly at ub.
241 end = (pos == max_intervals - 1) ? ub : lb + (pos + 1) * base_interval;
242 } else {
243 // For Backward: first interval gets ub exactly, last gets lb
244 // exactly.
245 start = (pos == max_intervals - 1) ? lb : ub - (pos + 1) * base_interval;
246 end = (pos == 0) ? ub : ub - pos * base_interval;
247 }
248 start = roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(start);
249 end = roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(end);
250 return {start, end};
251 };
252
253 int cum_sum = 0;
254 int bucket_index = 0;
255 std::vector<double> interval_start(num_intervals);
256 std::vector<double> interval_end(num_intervals);
257 std::vector<int> pos(num_intervals,
258 0); // combination indices for multi-dimensions
259
260 // 4. Process each node to define new buckets.
261 for (const auto &vrp_node : nodes) {
262 std::vector<int> node_split_counts(num_intervals);
263 std::vector<double> node_base_interval(num_intervals);
264 // Determine the number of splits and base interval for each
265 // resource dimension.
266 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
267 double full_range = R_max[r] - R_min[r];
268 double node_range = vrp_node.ub[r] - vrp_node.lb[r];
269 int splits = 1;
270 if (std::fabs(full_range) > std::numeric_limits<double>::epsilon()) {
271 double splits_d = (node_range * intervals[r].interval) / full_range;
272 splits = std::max(1, static_cast<int>(std::round(splits_d)));
273 }
274 node_split_counts[r] = splits;
275 node_base_interval[r] = (vrp_node.ub[r] - vrp_node.lb[r]) / static_cast<double>(splits);
276 }
277 // Optionally store node-specific split counts.
278 bucket_splits[vrp_node.id] = node_split_counts;
279
280 int n_buckets = 0;
281 if (num_intervals == 1) {
282 // Single-dimensional case.
283 for (int j = 0; j < node_split_counts[0]; ++j) {
284 auto [int_start, int_end] = calculate_interval(vrp_node.lb[0], vrp_node.ub[0], node_base_interval[0], j,
285 node_split_counts[0], D == Direction::Forward);
286 if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward)
287 int_start = std::max(int_start, vrp_node.lb[0]);
288 else
289 int_end = std::min(int_end, vrp_node.ub[0]);
290 buckets.push_back(Bucket(vrp_node.id, std::vector<double>{int_start}, std::vector<double>{int_end}));
291 n_buckets++;
292 cum_sum++;
293 }
294 } else {
295 // Multi-dimensional: iterate over all combinations.
296 fmt::print("Multiple intervals\n");
297 std::fill(pos.begin(), pos.end(), 0);
298 while (true) {
299 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
300 auto [int_start, int_end] =
301 calculate_interval(vrp_node.lb[r], vrp_node.ub[r], node_base_interval[r], pos[r],
302 node_split_counts[r], D == Direction::Forward);
303 interval_start[r] = int_start;
304 interval_end[r] = int_end;
305 if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward)
306 interval_start[r] = std::max(interval_start[r], R_min[r]);
307 else
308 interval_end[r] = std::min(interval_end[r], R_max[r]);
309 }
310 buckets.push_back(Bucket(vrp_node.id, interval_start, interval_end));
311 n_buckets++;
312 cum_sum++;
313
314 // Generate next combination.
315 int i = 0;
316 while (i < num_intervals) {
317 ++pos[i];
318 if (pos[i] < node_split_counts[i])
319 break;
320 else {
321 pos[i] = 0;
322 ++i;
323 }
324 }
325 if (i == num_intervals) break;
326 }
327 }
328 num_buckets[vrp_node.id] = n_buckets;
329 num_buckets_index[vrp_node.id] = cum_sum - n_buckets;
330 // Optionally assign node_interval_trees[vrp_node.id] = node_tree;
331 //
332 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) { n_segments = (n_buckets + 63) >> 6; }
333 }
334
335 // 5. Process fixed bucket ranges (if available).
336 if (!node_pair_fixed_ranges.empty()) {
337 size_t new_size = buckets.size();
338 size_t required_bitmap_words = std::max(size_t(1), ((new_size * new_size) + 63) / 64);
339 fixed_buckets_bitmap.clear();
340 fixed_buckets_bitmap.resize(required_bitmap_words, 0);
341 for (size_t from = 0; from < new_size; ++from) {
342 const auto &bucket_from = buckets[from];
343 int from_node = bucket_from.node_id;
344 if (node_pair_fixed_ranges.find(from_node) == node_pair_fixed_ranges.end()) continue;
345 for (size_t to = 0; to < new_size; ++to) {
346 const auto &bucket_to = buckets[to];
347 int to_node = bucket_to.node_id;
348 if (node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node].find(to_node) == node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node].end())
349 continue;
350 const auto &range = node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node][to_node];
351 bool in_range = true;
352 for (int r = 0; r < options.main_resources.size(); ++r) {
353 if (bucket_from.lb[r] < range.first[r] || bucket_from.ub[r] > range.second[r]) {
354 in_range = false;
355 break;
356 }
357 }
358 if (in_range) {
359 size_t bit_pos = from * new_size + to;
360 size_t word_idx = bit_pos / 64;
361 if (word_idx < fixed_buckets_bitmap.size()) {
362 fixed_buckets_bitmap[word_idx] |= (1ULL << (bit_pos % 64));
363 }
364 }
365 }
366 }
367 }
368
369 // 6. Finalize: record the total bucket count.
370 buckets_size = buckets.size();
371}

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::define_buckets ( const int fixed_num_buckets)
inline

1307 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
1308 buckets.clear();
1309

1311 auto &fixed_buckets_bitmap = assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets_bitmap, bw_fixed_buckets_bitmap);
1312 fixed_buckets_bitmap.clear();
1313
1314 // Get other direction-specific containers.

1344
1345 int bucket_index = 0;
1346 int cum_sum = 0;
1347 std::vector<double> interval_start(num_intervals);
1348 std::vector<double> interval_end(num_intervals);
1349 // "pos" holds the current combination indices when
1350 // multi-dimensional.
1351 std::vector<int> pos(num_intervals, 0);
1352
1353 // Process each node (job) to define its buckets.
1354 for (const auto &VRPNode : nodes) {
1355 // Compute the number of splits for each resource dimension.
1356 // For a single dimension, we use the fixed number directly.
1357 // For multiple dimensions we choose an equal split in each
1358 // dimension.
1359 std::vector<int> node_split_counts(num_intervals, 1);
1360 if (num_intervals == 1) {
1361 node_split_counts[0] = fixed_num_buckets;
1362 } else {
1363 // Compute an equal split count per dimension (rounded).
1364 int equal_split =
1365 std::max(1, static_cast<int>(std::round(std::pow(fixed_num_buckets, 1.0 / num_intervals))));
1366 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) { node_split_counts[r] = equal_split; }
1367 // Adjust the first dimension if the product is less than
1368 // fixed_num_buckets.
1369 int product = 1;
1370 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) { product *= node_split_counts[r]; }
1371 if (product < fixed_num_buckets) {
1372 int factor = fixed_num_buckets / product;
1373 node_split_counts[0] *= factor;
1374 }
1375 }
1376
1377 // Compute the width (base interval) for each resource
1378 // dimension.
1379 std::vector<double> node_base_interval(num_intervals);
1380 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
1381 node_base_interval[r] = (VRPNode.ub[r] - VRPNode.lb[r]) / static_cast<double>(node_split_counts[r]);
1382 }
1383
1384 // Optionally store the per-node split information.
1385 // (Here we store just the first dimension's split count; you
1386 // could store the full vector.)
1387 bucket_splits[VRPNode.id] = (num_intervals == 1) ? node_split_counts[0] : node_split_counts[0];
1388
1389 SplayTree node_tree;
1390 int n_buckets = 0;
1391
1392 if (num_intervals == 1) {
1393 // Single-dimensional splitting.
1394 for (int j = 0; j < node_split_counts[0]; ++j) {
1395 auto [start, end] = calculate_interval(VRPNode.lb[0], VRPNode.ub[0], node_base_interval[0], j,
1396 node_split_counts[0], D == Direction::Forward);
1397 // Clamp to the node's bounds.
1398 if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward) {
1399 start = std::max(start, VRPNode.lb[0]);
1400 } else {
1401 end = std::min(end, VRPNode.ub[0]);
1402 }
1403 buckets.push_back(Bucket(VRPNode.id, std::vector<double>{start}, std::vector<double>{end}));
1404 node_tree.insert(std::vector<double>{start}, std::vector<double>{end}, bucket_index++);
1405 n_buckets++;
1406 cum_sum++;
1407 }
1408 } else {
1409 // Multi-dimensional splitting.
1410 std::fill(pos.begin(), pos.end(), 0);
1411 while (true) {
1412 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
1413 auto [start, end] = calculate_interval(VRPNode.lb[r], VRPNode.ub[r], node_base_interval[r],
1414 pos[r], node_split_counts[r], D == Direction::Forward);
1415 interval_start[r] = start;
1416 interval_end[r] = end;
1417 // Optionally clamp to global resource bounds.
1418 if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward) {
1419 interval_start[r] = std::max(interval_start[r], R_min[r]);
1420 } else {
1421 interval_end[r] = std::min(interval_end[r], R_max[r]);
1422 }
1423 }
1424 buckets.push_back(Bucket(VRPNode.id, interval_start, interval_end));
1425 node_tree.insert(interval_start, interval_end, bucket_index++);
1426 n_buckets++;
1427 cum_sum++;
1428
1429 // Generate next combination of splits.
1430 int i = 0;
1431 while (i < num_intervals) {
1432 ++pos[i];
1433 if (pos[i] < node_split_counts[i]) {
1434 break;
1435 } else {
1436 pos[i] = 0;
1437 ++i;
1438 }
1439 }
1440 if (i == num_intervals) break;
1441 }
1442 }
1443
1444 // Update per-node bucket bookkeeping.
1445 num_buckets[VRPNode.id] = n_buckets;
1446 num_buckets_index[VRPNode.id] = cum_sum - n_buckets;
1447 node_interval_trees[VRPNode.id] = node_tree;
1448 }
1449
1450 // Update the overall bucket count.
1451 buckets_size = buckets.size();
1452 }
SplayTree::insert
void insert(const std::vector< double > &low, const std::vector< double > &high, int bucket_index)

void BucketGraph::define_elementarity_sets ( )
inline

Definition at line 374 of file BucketGraph.h.

374 {
375 size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
376
377 // Initialize elementarity sets
378 elementarity_bitmap.resize(num_nodes); // Bitmap for elementarity sets
379
380 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
381 // Each customer should appear in its own elementarity
382 // set
383 size_t num_segments = (num_nodes + 63) / 64;
384 elementarity_bitmap[i].resize(num_segments, 0);
385
386 // Mark the node itself in the elementarity set
387 size_t segment_self = i >> 6;
388 size_t bit_position_self = i & 63;
389 elementarity_bitmap[i][segment_self] |= (1ULL << bit_position_self);
390 }
391 }

393 {
394 size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
395
396 // Initialize packing sets
397 packing_bitmap.resize(num_nodes); // Bitmap for packing sets
398
399 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
400 // Each customer should appear in its own packing set
401 size_t num_segments = (num_nodes + 63) / 64;
402 packing_bitmap[i].resize(num_segments, 0);
403
404 // Mark the node itself in the packing set
405 size_t segment_self = i >> 6;
406 size_t bit_position_self = i & 63;
407 packing_bitmap[i][segment_self] |= (1ULL << bit_position_self);
408 }

◆ DominatedInCompWiseSmallerBuckets() [1/2]

template<Direction D, Stage S>
bool BucketGraph::DominatedInCompWiseSmallerBuckets ( const Label *__restrict__ L,
int bucket,
const std::vector< double > &__restrict__ c_bar,
std::vector< uint64_t > &__restrict__ Bvisited,
std::vector< uint32_t > & touched_segments,
uint & stat_n_dom )
inlinenoexcept

Determines if a label is dominated in component-wise smaller buckets.

This function checks if a given label is dominated by any other label in component-wise smaller buckets. The dominance is determined based on the cost and order of the buckets.

Definition at line 1144 of file BucketSolve.h.

1148 {
1149 // Cache direction-specific data
1150 const auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
1151 const auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
1152 const auto &bucket_scc_rank = assign_buckets<D>(fw_bucket_scc_rank, bw_bucket_scc_rank);
1153
1154 // Cache frequently used label properties
1155 const int b_L = L->vertex;
1156 const double label_cost = L->cost;
1157 const int label_rank = bucket_scc_rank[b_L];
1158
1159 // Use static thread-local stack to avoid repeated allocations
1160 static thread_local std::vector<int> stack_buffer;
1161 if (stack_buffer.capacity() < R_SIZE) { stack_buffer.reserve(R_SIZE); }
1162 stack_buffer.clear();
1163 stack_buffer.push_back(bucket);
1164
1165 // Direct pointer to bit mask lookup for fast bit operations
1166 const uint64_t *const bit_mask_lookup_ptr = bit_mask_lookup.data();
1167
1168 // Inline dominance check function to avoid lambda overhead
1169 auto inline_check_dominance = [&](std::span<Label *const> labels, uint &n_dom) -> bool {
1170#ifdef __AVX2__
1171 if (labels.size() >= AVX_LIM)
1172 return check_dominance_against_vector<D, S>(L, labels, cut_storage, n_dom);
1173#endif
1174 const size_t size = labels.size();
1175 size_t i = 0;
1176 // Loop unrolling: process 4 labels at a time
1177 for (; i + 3 < size; i += 4) {
1178 if (is_dominated<D, S>(L, labels[i]) || is_dominated<D, S>(L, labels[i + 1]) ||
1179 is_dominated<D, S>(L, labels[i + 2]) || is_dominated<D, S>(L, labels[i + 3])) {
1180 ++n_dom;
1181 return true;
1182 }
1183 }
1184 // Process remaining labels
1185 for (; i < size; ++i) {
1186 if (is_dominated<D, S>(L, labels[i])) {
1187 ++n_dom;
1188 return true;
1189 }
1190 }
1191 return false;
1192 };
1193
1194 // Main DFS traversal loop
1195 while (!stack_buffer.empty()) {
1196 const int current_bucket = stack_buffer.back();
1197 stack_buffer.pop_back();
1198
1199 const size_t segment = static_cast<size_t>(current_bucket) >> 6;
1200 const uint64_t bit = bit_mask_lookup_ptr[current_bucket & 63];
1201
1202 if (unlikely(Bvisited[segment] & bit)) continue;
1203
1204 // Mark as visited
1205 if (Bvisited[segment] == 0) { touched_segments.push_back(static_cast<uint32_t>(segment)); }
1206 Bvisited[segment] |= bit;
1207
1208 // Fast path: if label cost is lower and bucket precedes L in the SCC
1209 // order, return early
1210 if (likely(label_cost < c_bar[current_bucket] && bucket_scc_rank[current_bucket] < label_rank))
1211 return false;
1212
1213 // Skip dominance check for L's own bucket
1214 if (b_L != current_bucket) {
1215 const auto &mother_bucket = buckets[current_bucket];
1216 if (mother_bucket.check_dominance(L, inline_check_dominance, stat_n_dom)) return true;
1217 }
1218
1219 // Process neighbor buckets using pointer arithmetic and prefetching
1220 const auto &neighbors = Phi[current_bucket];
1221 const size_t n_neighbors = neighbors.size();
1222 const int *neighbor_ptr = neighbors.data();
1223 const int *neighbor_end = neighbor_ptr + n_neighbors;
1224 for (; neighbor_ptr != neighbor_end; ++neighbor_ptr) {
1225 const int b_prime = *neighbor_ptr;
1226 const size_t word_idx = static_cast<size_t>(b_prime) >> 6;
1227 const uint64_t mask = bit_mask_lookup_ptr[b_prime & 63];
1228 __builtin_prefetch(&Bvisited[word_idx], 0,
1229 1); // Prefetch Bvisited for this segment
1230 if (!(Bvisited[word_idx] & mask)) stack_buffer.push_back(b_prime);
1231 }
1232 }
1233
1234 return false;
1235}

template<Direction D, Stage S, ArcType A, Mutability M, Full F>
auto BucketGraph::Extend ( const std::conditional_t< M==Mutability::Mut, Label *, const Label * > L_prime,
const std::conditional_t< A==ArcType::Bucket, BucketArc, std::conditional_t< A==ArcType::Jump, JumpArc, Arc > > & gamma,

inlinenoexcept

Extends the label L_prime with the given BucketArc gamma.

Definition at line 722 of file BucketSolve.h.

725 {
726 // Pre-allocated result vector to avoid allocations for the common case of
727 // 0-1 results
728 static thread_local std::vector<Label *> result_vector;
729 Label *result = nullptr;
730
731 // Prepare temporary resource storage (resize to current resources size)
732 static thread_local std::vector<double> new_resources;
733 const size_t n_resources = options.resources.size();
734 new_resources.resize(n_resources);
735
736 // Cache initial values from L_prime
737 const int initial_node_id = L_prime->node_id;
738 const auto &initial_resources = L_prime->resources; // Use reference instead of copy
739 const double initial_cost = L_prime->cost;
740
741 // Determine target node ID based on arc type
742 int node_id;
743 if constexpr (A == ArcType::Bucket) {
744 node_id = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets)[gamma.to_bucket].node_id;
745 } else if constexpr (A == ArcType::Node) {
746 node_id = gamma.to;
747 } else { // Jump arc
748 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
749 node_id = buckets[gamma.jump_bucket].node_id;
750 const auto &bucket = buckets[gamma.jump_bucket];
751
752 // Fast copy the resources
753 std::memcpy(new_resources.data(), initial_resources.data(), n_resources * sizeof(double));
754
755 // Update resource bounds based on jump bucket properties
756 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_resources; ++i) {
757 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
758 new_resources[i] = std::max(new_resources[i], static_cast<double>(bucket.lb[i]));
759 } else {
760 new_resources[i] = std::min(new_resources[i], static_cast<double>(bucket.ub[i]));
761 }
762 }
763 }
764
765 // Early rejection: if we loop back or the node was already visited
766 if (node_id == initial_node_id || is_node_visited(L_prime->visited_bitmap, node_id)) {
767 if constexpr (F == Full::Reverse)
768 return -1;
769 else
770 return result;
771 }
772
773 // For stages where fixed arcs matter
774 if constexpr (S >= Stage::Three || S == Stage::Eliminate) {
775 if ((D == Direction::Forward && is_arc_fixed(initial_node_id, node_id)) ||
776 (D == Direction::Backward && is_arc_fixed(node_id, initial_node_id))) {
777 if constexpr (F == Full::Reverse)
778 return -1;
779 else
780 return result;
781 }
782 }
783
784 // Process resources (feasibility check)
785 if constexpr (F != Full::TSP) {
786 if constexpr (A != ArcType::Jump) {
787 // Only copy resources for non-jump arcs (jump arcs already copied)
788 std::memcpy(new_resources.data(), initial_resources.data(), n_resources * sizeof(double));
789
790 if (!process_all_resources<D>(new_resources, initial_resources, gamma, nodes[node_id], n_resources)) {
791 if constexpr (F == Full::Reverse)
792 return -1;
793 else
794 return result;
795 }
796 } else {
797 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
798 const auto &bucket = buckets[gamma.jump_bucket];
799 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_resources; ++i) {
800 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
801 new_resources[i] =
802 std::max(new_resources[i] + gamma.resource_increment[i], static_cast<double>(bucket.lb[i]));
803 if (numericutils::gt(new_resources[i], bucket.ub[i])) {
804 if constexpr (F == Full::Reverse)
805 return -1;
806 else
807 return nullptr;
808 }
809
810 } else {
811 new_resources[i] =
812 std::min(new_resources[i] - gamma.resource_increment[i], static_cast<double>(bucket.ub[i]));
813 if (numericutils::lt(new_resources[i], bucket.lb[i])) {
814 if constexpr (F == Full::Reverse)
815 return -1;
816 else
817 return nullptr;
818 }
819 }
820 }
821 }
822 }
823
824 // For PSTEP/TSP: Check path length constraints
825 if constexpr (F == Full::PSTEP || F == Full::TSP) {
826 const int next_path_len = L_prime->path_len + 1;
827 if (next_path_len > options.max_path_size ||
828 (next_path_len == options.max_path_size && node_id != options.end_depot)) {
829 if constexpr (F == Full::Reverse)
830 return -1;
831 else
832 return result;
833 }
834 }
835
836 // Get bucket number for the new label
837 int to_bucket = -1;
838 if constexpr (A == ArcType::Bucket) {
839 to_bucket = get_bucket_number<D>(node_id, new_resources);
840 } else if constexpr (A == ArcType::Jump) {
841 to_bucket = gamma.jump_bucket;
842 } else if constexpr (A == ArcType::Node) {
843 to_bucket = get_bucket_number<D>(node_id, new_resources);
844 }
845
846 // Handle Reverse mode early
847 if constexpr (F == Full::Reverse) {
848 if (new_resources[options.main_resources[0]] <= std::floor(q_star[options.main_resources[0]])) return -1;
849
850 return to_bucket;
851 }
852
853 // Calculate new cost
854#ifdef CUSTOM_COST
855 const auto distance = getcij(initial_node_id, node_id);
856 double new_cost = cost_calculator.calculate_cost(initial_cost, distance, node_id, new_resources);
857#else
858 // Cache the distance value to avoid repeated calls
859 const auto distance = getcij(initial_node_id, node_id);
860 double new_cost = initial_cost + distance;
861#endif
862
863 const auto &VRPNode = nodes[node_id];
864
865 if constexpr (F != Full::PSTEP) {
866 new_cost -= VRPNode.cost;
867 } else {
868 if (L_prime->path_len > 1 && initial_node_id != options.depot) {
869 const auto three_two_dual = pstep_duals.getThreeTwoDualValue(initial_node_id);
870 new_cost += three_two_dual + pstep_duals.getThreeThreeDualValue(initial_node_id);
871 }
872 new_cost += -pstep_duals.getThreeTwoDualValue(node_id) + pstep_duals.getArcDualValue(initial_node_id, node_id);
873 }
874
875 // Apply branching dual adjustments if present - use local variables to
876 // avoid repeated pointer dereferencing
877 if (!branching_duals->empty()) {
878 if (D == Direction::Forward) {
879 new_cost += branching_duals->getDual(initial_node_id, node_id);
880 } else {
881 new_cost += branching_duals->getDual(node_id, initial_node_id);
882 }
883 new_cost += branching_duals->getDual(node_id);
884 }
885
886 RCC_MODE_BLOCK(if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
887 if (D == Direction::Forward) {
888 new_cost -= arc_duals.getDual(initial_node_id, node_id);
889 } else {
890 new_cost -= arc_duals.getDual(node_id, initial_node_id);
891 }
892 })
893
894 // Create and initialize a new label
895 auto &label_pool = assign_buckets<D>(label_pool_fw, label_pool_bw);
896 auto new_label = label_pool->acquire();
897 new_label->initialize(to_bucket, new_cost, new_resources, node_id);
898 new_label->vertex = to_bucket;
899 new_label->real_cost = L_prime->real_cost + distance; // Use cached distance
900 new_label->path_len = L_prime->path_len + 1;
901
902 // Update visited bitmap: intersect parent's visited bits with neighborhood
903 // mask
904 if constexpr (F != Full::PSTEP) {
905 for (size_t i = 0; i < new_label->visited_bitmap.size(); ++i) {
906 const uint64_t current_visited = L_prime->visited_bitmap[i];
907 if (current_visited) { new_label->visited_bitmap[i] = current_visited & neighborhoods_bitmap[node_id][i]; }
908 }
909 } else {
910 // Use memcpy for direct bitmap copy (likely faster than assignment for
911 // large bitmaps)
912 std::memcpy(new_label->visited_bitmap.data(), L_prime->visited_bitmap.data(),
913 new_label->visited_bitmap.size() * sizeof(uint64_t));
914 }
915
916 set_node_visited(new_label->visited_bitmap, node_id);
917
918 // Reuse pooled vector capacity when extending the route.
919 auto &extended_route = new_label->nodes_covered;
920 extended_route.clear();
921 extended_route.reserve(L_prime->nodes_covered.size() + 1);
922 extended_route.insert(extended_route.end(), L_prime->nodes_covered.begin(), L_prime->nodes_covered.end());
923 extended_route.push_back(node_id);
924
925#if defined(SRC)
926 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four || S == Stage::Enumerate) {
927 new_label->SRCmap = L_prime->SRCmap;
928 auto &cutter = cut_storage;
929 const auto active_cuts = cutter->getActiveCuts();
930 const auto n_cuts = cutter->activeSize();
931 if (__builtin_expect(n_cuts == 0, 0)) { // Branch prediction: unlikely
932 if constexpr (F == Full::Reverse) {
933 return -1;
934 } else {
935 return new_label;
936 }
937 }
938
939 double total_cost_update = 0.0;
940 const size_t segment = node_id >> 6;
941 const size_t bit_position = node_id & 63;
942
943 // Prefetch critical data structures
944 auto &masks = cutter->getSegmentMasks();
945 __builtin_prefetch(&masks, 0, 3);
946 __builtin_prefetch(active_cuts.data(), 0, 3);
947 __builtin_prefetch(new_label->SRCmap.data(), 1, 3); // 1 = read-write
948
949 const auto cut_limit_mask = masks.get_cut_limit_mask();
950 const uint64_t valid_cuts = masks.get_valid_cut_mask(segment, bit_position) & cut_limit_mask;
951
952 // Skip processing if no valid cuts
953 if (__builtin_expect(valid_cuts != 0,
954 1)) { // Branch prediction: likely
955 // Process valid cuts (4 at a time if possible)
956 uint64_t remaining_cuts = valid_cuts;
957
958 // Process blocks of 4 cuts if available (manual loop unrolling)
959 while (__builtin_popcountll(remaining_cuts) >= 4) {
960 for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++) {
961 const int cut_idx = __builtin_ctzll(remaining_cuts);
962 const auto &active_cut = active_cuts[cut_idx];
963 const auto &cut = *active_cut.cut_ptr;
964
965 // Prefetch the next cut data we'll need
966 if (i < 3) {
967 uint64_t next_bits = remaining_cuts & (remaining_cuts - 1);
968 const int next_cut_idx = __builtin_ctzll(next_bits);
969 __builtin_prefetch(&active_cuts[next_cut_idx], 0, 3);
970 __builtin_prefetch(active_cuts[next_cut_idx].cut_ptr, 0, 3);
971 }
972
973 auto &src_map_value = new_label->SRCmap[active_cut.index];
974 const double num_value = cut.p.num[cut.baseSetOrder[node_id]];
975 src_map_value += num_value;
976
977 // Use branchless version when possible
978 const bool overflow = src_map_value >= cut.p.den;
979 src_map_value -= overflow ? cut.p.den : 0;
980 total_cost_update -= overflow ? active_cut.dual_value : 0;
981
982 remaining_cuts &= (remaining_cuts - 1); // Clear processed bit
983 }
984 }
985
986 // Process remaining cuts (less than 4)
987 while (remaining_cuts) {
988 const int cut_idx = __builtin_ctzll(remaining_cuts);
989 const auto &active_cut = active_cuts[cut_idx];
990 const auto &cut = *active_cut.cut_ptr;
991
992 auto &src_map_value = new_label->SRCmap[active_cut.index];
993 src_map_value += cut.p.num[cut.baseSetOrder[node_id]];
994 if (src_map_value >= cut.p.den) {
995 src_map_value -= cut.p.den;
996 total_cost_update -= active_cut.dual_value;
997 }
998 remaining_cuts &= (remaining_cuts - 1);
999 }
1000 }
1001
1002 // Process cuts to clear
1003 uint64_t to_clear = (~masks.get_neighbor_mask(segment, bit_position)) & cut_limit_mask;
1004
1005 if (__builtin_expect(to_clear != 0, 1)) { // Branch prediction: likely
1006 // Use vectorization for clearing multiple cuts when possible
1007 constexpr int vector_size = 8; // Process 8 cuts at a time if possible
1008
1009 while (__builtin_popcountll(to_clear) >= vector_size) {
1010 for (int i = 0; i < vector_size; i++) {
1011 const int cut_idx = __builtin_ctzll(to_clear);
1012 new_label->SRCmap[active_cuts[cut_idx].index] = 0.0;
1013 to_clear &= (to_clear - 1);
1014 }
1015 }
1016
1017 // Process remaining cuts to clear
1018 while (to_clear) {
1019 const int cut_idx = __builtin_ctzll(to_clear);
1020 new_label->SRCmap[active_cuts[cut_idx].index] = 0.0;
1021 to_clear &= (to_clear - 1);
1022 }
1023 }
1024
1025 new_label->cost += total_cost_update;
1026 }
1027#endif
1028 if constexpr (F != Full::Reverse) {
1029 return new_label;
1030 } else {
1031 return -1;
1032 }
1033}
