◆ forbidCycle()

void BucketGraph::forbidCycle ( const std::vector< int > & cycle,
bool aggressive )

Forbids a cycle in the bucket graph.

This function takes a vector representing a cycle in the graph and forbids the edges corresponding to the cycle. If the 'aggressive' flag is set to true, it also forbids additional edges between the vertices of the cycle.

Definition at line 456 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

456 {
457 // If the cycle is too short, there is nothing to forbid.
458 if (cycle.size() < 2) return;
459
460 // Process each adjacent pair in the cycle.
461 for (size_t i = 0; i < cycle.size() - 1; ++i) {
462 int v1 = cycle[i];
463 int v2 = cycle[i + 1];
464
465 // Forbid v2 in the neighborhood of v1.
466 size_t segment = static_cast<size_t>(v2) >> 6;
467 size_t bit_position = static_cast<size_t>(v2) & 63;
468 neighborhoods_bitmap[v1][segment] |= bit_mask_lookup[bit_position];
469
470 if (aggressive) {
471 // Additionally, forbid v1 in the neighborhood of v2.
472 segment = static_cast<size_t>(v1) >> 6;
473 bit_position = static_cast<size_t>(v1) & 63;
474 neighborhoods_bitmap[v2][segment] |= bit_mask_lookup[bit_position];
475 }
476 }
477}

◆ generate_arcs() [1/2]

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::generate_arcs ( )

Generates arcs in the bucket graph based on the specified direction.

Generates arcs in the bucket graph based on the specified direction.

Definition at line 381 of file BucketUtils.h.

381 {
382 // Mutex for protecting global bucket updates.
383 std::mutex buckets_mutex;
384
385 // Clear the appropriate graph.
386 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
387 fw_bucket_graph.clear();
388 } else {
389 bw_bucket_graph.clear();
390 }
391
392 // Resolve references based on the direction.
393 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
394 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
395 auto &num_buckets_idx = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
396 // Note: we no longer rely solely on bucket_splits here.
397
398 // Pre-compute intervals (base widths) for each node and each resource,
399 // using the same logic as in define_buckets.
400 const int num_resources = options.resources.size();
401 std::vector<std::vector<double>> node_base_intervals(nodes.size());
402 // Also store the computed splits per resource for each node.
403 std::vector<std::vector<int>> node_split_counts(nodes.size());
404
405 for (size_t node_id = 0; node_id < nodes.size(); ++node_id) {
406 const auto &node = nodes[node_id];
407 node_base_intervals[node_id].resize(num_resources);
408 node_split_counts[node_id].resize(num_resources);
409 for (int r = 0; r < num_resources; ++r) {
410 double full_range = R_max[r] - R_min[r];
411 double node_range = node.ub[r] - node.lb[r];
412 int splits = 1;
413 if (std::fabs(full_range) > std::numeric_limits<double>::epsilon()) {
414 double splits_d = (node_range * intervals[r].interval) / full_range;
415 splits = std::max(1, static_cast<int>(std::round(splits_d)));
416 }
417 node_split_counts[node_id][r] = splits;
418 node_base_intervals[node_id][r] = (node.ub[r] - node.lb[r]) / static_cast<double>(splits);
419 }
420 }
421
422 // Clear all buckets and their arcs.
423 for (auto &bucket : buckets) {
424 bucket.clear();
425 bucket.clear_arcs(D == Direction::Forward);
426 }
427
428 // Helper lambda: validate an arc and, if valid, add it.
429 auto try_add_arc = [&](const VRPNode &curr_node, int from_bucket, const VRPNode &next_node, int to_bucket,
430 const std::vector<double> &curr_node_base, const std::vector<double> &next_node_base,
431 const std::vector<double> &res_inc, double cost_inc) -> bool {
432 bool valid = true;
433 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
434 for (int r = 0; r < res_inc.size() && valid; ++r) {
435 // For forward, check that adding the resource increment
436 // from the from-bucket does not push us past the next
437 // node's upper bound.
438 if (numericutils::gt(buckets[from_bucket].lb[r] + res_inc[r], next_node.ub[r])) {
439 valid = false;
440 } else {
441 double max_calc = std::max(buckets[from_bucket].lb[r] + res_inc[r], next_node.lb[r]);
442 if (numericutils::lt(max_calc, buckets[to_bucket].lb[r]) ||
443 numericutils::gte(max_calc, buckets[to_bucket].lb[r] + next_node_base[r])) {
444 valid = false;
445 }
446 }
447 }
448 } else {
449 for (int r = 0; r < res_inc.size() && valid; ++r) {
450 // For backward, check that subtracting the resource
451 // increment does not drop below next node's lb.
452 if (numericutils::lt(buckets[from_bucket].ub[r] - res_inc[r], next_node.lb[r])) {
453 valid = false;
454 } else {
455 double min_calc = std::min(buckets[from_bucket].ub[r] - res_inc[r], next_node.ub[r]);
456 if (numericutils::gt(min_calc, buckets[to_bucket].ub[r]) ||
457 numericutils::lte(min_calc, buckets[to_bucket].ub[r] - next_node_base[r])) {
458 valid = false;
459 }
460 }
461 }
462 }
463 if (valid) {
464 // Add the arc to the global structure.
465 {
466 std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(buckets_mutex);
467 add_arc<D>(from_bucket, to_bucket, res_inc, cost_inc);
468 buckets[from_bucket].template add_bucket_arc<D>(from_bucket, to_bucket, res_inc, cost_inc, false);
469 }
470 }
471 return valid;
472 };
473
474 // Lambda to process a single node.
475 auto process_node = [&](int node_id) {
476 const auto &node = nodes[node_id];
477 std::vector<double> res_inc(num_resources);
478 // Local container for arcs (if further processing is needed).
479 std::vector<std::pair<int, int>> local_arcs;
480 local_arcs.reserve(nodes.size());
481
482 const auto arcs = node.get_arcs<D>();
483 // Use the pre-computed base intervals for the current node.
484 const auto &curr_node_base = node_base_intervals[node.id];
485
486 // For every bucket in the current node.
487 for (int i = 0; i < num_buckets[node.id]; ++i) {
488 int from_bucket = i + num_buckets_idx[node.id];
489 // Process each outgoing arc.
490 for (const auto &arc : nodes) {
491 const auto &next_node = arc;
492 // Avoid self-loop.
493 if (node.id == next_node.id) continue;
494
495 const auto &next_node_base = node_base_intervals[next_node.id];
496 const double travel_cost = getcij(node.id, next_node.id);
497 double cost_inc = travel_cost - next_node.cost;
498 // Pre-calculate resource increments.
499 for (int r = 0; r < num_resources; r++) {
500 res_inc[r] = node.consumption[r];
501 if (options.resources[r] == "time") { res_inc[r] += travel_cost; }
502 }
503
504 // Iterate over buckets in the next node.
505 for (int j = 0; j < num_buckets[next_node.id]; ++j) {
506 int to_bucket = j + num_buckets_idx[next_node.id];
507 // if (from_bucket == to_bucket ||
508 // is_bucket_fixed<D>(from_bucket, to_bucket)) {
509 // continue;
510 // }
511 if (from_bucket == to_bucket) { continue; }
512 // // Validate and add arc if valid.
513 if (try_add_arc(node, from_bucket, next_node, to_bucket, curr_node_base, next_node_base, res_inc,
514 cost_inc)) {
515 local_arcs.emplace_back(from_bucket, to_bucket);
516 }
517 }
518 }
519 }
520 // If needed, local_arcs can be used for debugging or further
521 // processing.
522 };
523
524 // Create a list of tasks (each task is a node index).
525 std::vector<int> tasks(nodes.size());
526 std::iota(tasks.begin(), tasks.end(), 0);
527
528 // Choose a chunk size based on a heuristic.
529 constexpr int chunk_size = 10;
530
531 // Set up the thread pool.
532 const unsigned int thread_count = std::max(1u, std::thread::hardware_concurrency() / 2);
533 exec::static_thread_pool pool(thread_count);
534 auto sched = pool.get_scheduler();
535
536 // Launch tasks in parallel over chunks.
537 auto bulk_sender = stdexec::bulk(stdexec::just(), (tasks.size() + chunk_size - 1) / chunk_size,
538 [&, chunk_size](std::size_t chunk_idx) {
539 const size_t start_idx = chunk_idx * chunk_size;
540 const size_t end_idx = std::min(start_idx + chunk_size, tasks.size());
541 for (size_t idx = start_idx; idx < end_idx; ++idx) {
542 process_node(tasks[idx]);
543 }
544 });
545
546 auto work = stdexec::starts_on(sched, bulk_sender);
547 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(work));
548}
BucketGraph::add_arc
void add_arc(int from_bucket, int to_bucket, const std::vector< double > &res_inc, double cost_inc)
Represents a bucket in the Bucket Graph. Adds an arc to the bucket graph.
Definition BucketUtils.h:71

◆ generate_arcs() [2/2]

void BucketGraph::generate_arcs ( )

Generates arcs in both forward and backward directions in parallel.

This function uses OpenMP to parallelize the generation of arcs in both forward and backward directions. It performs the following steps for each direction:

  • Calls the generate_arcs function template with the appropriate direction.
  • Clears and resizes the Phi vector for the respective direction.
  • Computes the Phi values for each bucket and stores them in the Phi vector.
  • Calls the SCC_handler function template with the appropriate direction.

The forward direction operations are performed in one OpenMP section, and the backward direction operations are performed in another OpenMP section.

Definition at line 779 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

779 {
780 PARALLEL_SECTIONS(
781 work, bi_sched,
782 SECTION {
783 // Task for Forward Direction
784 generate_arcs<Direction::Forward>();
785 Phi_fw.clear();
786 Phi_fw.resize(fw_buckets_size);
787 for (int i = 0; i < fw_buckets_size; ++i) { Phi_fw[i] = computePhi(i, true); }
788 SCC_handler<Direction::Forward>();
789 },
790 SECTION {
791 // Task for Backward Direction
792 generate_arcs<Direction::Backward>();
793 Phi_bw.clear();
794 Phi_bw.resize(bw_buckets_size);
795 for (int i = 0; i < bw_buckets_size; ++i) { Phi_bw[i] = computePhi(i, false); }
796 SCC_handler<Direction::Backward>();
797 });
798}

template<Symmetry SYM>
AdjList BucketGraph::get_adjacency_list ( )
inline

Definition at line 1218 of file BucketGraph.h.

1218 {
1219 AdjList adjacency_list;
1220
1221 // Iterate through all nodes in the graph
1222 for (const auto &node : nodes) {
1223 std::vector<std::tuple<int, double, int>> arcs;
1224
1225 // Retrieve all arcs associated with the current node
1226 for (const auto &arc : node.get_arcs<Direction::Forward>()) {
1227 int to_node = arc.to;
1228 double cost = getcij(node.id, to_node) - nodes[to_node].cost;
1229 int capacity = 1;
1230
1231 // Only add arcs with non-zero capacity
1232 if (capacity > 0) { arcs.emplace_back(to_node, cost, capacity); }
1233 }
1234
1235 // Add the node's arcs to the adjacency list if it has
1236 // any outgoing edges
1237 if (!arcs.empty()) { adjacency_list[node.id] = arcs; }
1238 }
1239
1240 return adjacency_list;
1241 }

◆ get_best_label()

template<Direction D>
Label * BucketGraph::get_best_label ( const std::vector< int > & topological_order,
const std::vector< double > & c_bar,
const std::vector< std::vector< int > > & sccs )

Retrieves the best label from the bucket graph based on the given topological order, c_bar values, and strongly connected components.

This function iterates through the given topological order and for each component, it retrieves the labels from the corresponding buckets in the bucket graph. It then compares the cost of each label and keeps track of the label with the lowest cost. The best label, along with its associated bucket, is returned.

Definition at line 562 of file BucketUtils.h.

563 {
564 double best_cost = std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
565 Label *best_label = nullptr;
566 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
567
568 // Iterate over all buckets, and select the label with the lowest cost.
569 for (auto &bucket : buckets) {
570 Label *label = bucket.get_best_label();
571 if (!label) continue;
572 if (label->cost < best_cost) {
573 best_cost = label->cost;
574 best_label = label;
575 }
576 }
577 return best_label;
578}

template<Direction D>
int BucketGraph::get_bucket_number ( int node,
std::vector< double > & values )
inlinenoexcept

Definition at line 83 of file BucketUtils.h.

83 {
84 const size_t num_resources = options.main_resources.size();
85
86 const auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
87
88 // Optionally adjust resource values (uncomment if you need epsilon
89 // adjustments)
90 for (size_t r = 0; r < num_resources; ++r) {
91 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
92 // resource_values_vec[r] += numericutils::eps;
93 } else { // Direction::Backward
94 // resource_values_vec[r] -= numericutils::eps;
95 }
96 }
97 auto &num_buckets_index = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
98 // Start with the offset for this node’s buckets.
99 int bucket_number = num_buckets_index[node];
100 int multiplier = 1;
101
102 for (size_t r = 0; r < num_resources; ++r) {
103 // Node-specific resource bounds.
104 double node_lb = nodes[node].lb[r];
105 double node_ub = nodes[node].ub[r];
106
107 // Global bounds (if needed for computing splits).
108 double global_lb = R_min[r];
109 double global_ub = R_max[r];
110 double full_range = global_ub - global_lb;
111 double node_range = node_ub - node_lb;
112
113 // Determine the number of splits (i.e. intervals) for resource r.
114 int splits = std::max(1, static_cast<int>(std::round((node_range / full_range) * intervals[r].interval)));
115 double interval_width = node_range / splits;
116
117 int pos = 0;
118 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
119 // Compute the bucket "digit" from the lower bound.
120 pos =
121 std::min(splits - 1, static_cast<int>(std::floor((resource_values_vec[r] - node_lb) / interval_width)));
122 } else { // Direction::Backward
123 // For backward, we consider the distance from the upper bound.
124 pos =
125 std::min(splits - 1, static_cast<int>(std::floor((node_ub - resource_values_vec[r]) / interval_width)));
126 }
127
128 bucket_number += pos * multiplier;
129 multiplier *= splits;
130 }
131
132 // check if buckets[bucket_number] contains the resource_vec
133 if (unlikely(!buckets[bucket_number].contains(resource_values_vec))) {
134 // print resource_values_vec
135 for (auto val : resource_values_vec) { fmt::print("{}\n", val); }
136 // print bucket bounds
137 fmt::print("{}\n", buckets[bucket_number].lb[0]);
138 fmt::print("{}\n", buckets[bucket_number].ub[0]);
139
140 std::throw_with_nested(std::runtime_error("Resource values not contained in bucket"));
141 return -1;
142 }
143
144 return bucket_number;
145}

◆ get_opposite_bucket_number()

template<Direction D>
int BucketGraph::get_opposite_bucket_number ( int current_bucket_index,
std::vector< double > & inc )

Get the opposite bucket number for a given bucket index.

This function retrieves the opposite bucket number based on the current bucket index and the specified direction. It determines the node and bounds of the current bucket, then calculates the opposite bucket index using the appropriate direction.

Definition at line 885 of file BucketUtils.h.

885 {
886 // Retrieve the current bucket based on the current direction.
887 const auto &current_bucket =
888 (D == Direction::Forward) ? fw_buckets[current_bucket_index] : bw_buckets[current_bucket_index];
889 const int node = current_bucket.node_id;
890 const auto &theNode = nodes[node];
891
892 // Pre-compute the reference point for the given node using the main
893 // resources.
894 const size_t num_resources = options.main_resources.size();
895 // Optionally, add an assertion or check that inc has the expected size.
896 assert(inc.size() == num_resources && theNode.lb.size() == num_resources && theNode.ub.size() == num_resources);
897
898 std::vector<double> reference_point(num_resources);
899
900 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
901 // For Forward direction:
902 // Use the maximum of inc and the node's lower bounds.
903 // This ensures we do not fall below the allowed lower bound.
904 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_resources; ++i) {
905 reference_point[i] = std::max(inc[i], theNode.lb[i]);
906 // Optionally, adjust with epsilon if needed:
907 // reference_point[i] += numericutils::eps;
908 }
909 // Get the opposite bucket using the Backward tree.
910 return get_bucket_number<Direction::Backward>(node, reference_point);
911 } else {
912 // For Backward direction:
913 // Use the minimum of inc and the node's upper bounds.
914 // This ensures we do not exceed the allowed upper bound.
915 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_resources; ++i) {
916 reference_point[i] = std::min(inc[i], theNode.ub[i]);
917 // Optionally, adjust with epsilon if needed:
918 // reference_point[i] -= numericutils::eps;
919 }
920 // Get the opposite bucket using the Forward tree.
921 return get_bucket_number<Direction::Forward>(node, reference_point);
922 }
923}

489 {

◆ heuristic_fixing()

template<Stage S>
void BucketGraph::heuristic_fixing ( )

Applies heuristic fixing to the current solution.

This function modifies the current solution based on the heuristic fixing strategy using the provided vector of values.

Definition at line 1000 of file BucketUtils.h.

1000 {
1001 if (fixed) { return; }
1002
1003 // Reset and initialize state.
1004 reset_pool();
1005 reset_fixed();
1006 common_initialization();
1007
1008 // Pre-allocate c-bar vectors with infinity.
1009 std::vector<double> forward_cbar(fw_buckets.size(), std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
1010 std::vector<double> backward_cbar(bw_buckets.size(), std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
1011
1012 gap = std::ceil(incumbent - (relaxation + std::min(0.0, min_red_cost)));
1013
1014 // Run the appropriate labeling algorithms based on stage
1015 if (S == Stage::Three) {
1016 // Run the labeling algorithms for Stage::Two with Full::Partial mode.
1017 run_labeling_algorithms<Stage::Two, Full::Partial>(forward_cbar, backward_cbar);
1018 } else if (S == Stage::Four) {
1019 fmt::print("\033[34m_STARTING ARC FIXING PROCEDURE\033[0m\n");
1020 fmt::print("gap: {}\n", gap);
1021 // Run the labeling algorithms for Stage::Four with Full::Full mode.
1022 run_labeling_algorithms<Stage::Four, Full::Full>(forward_cbar, backward_cbar);
1023 }
1024
1025 // Prepare containers for grouping labels by node.
1026 const size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
1027
1028 // Preallocate vectors with nullptr to avoid resizing
1029 std::vector<const Label *> min_fw_labels(num_nodes, nullptr);
1030 std::vector<const Label *> min_bw_labels(num_nodes, nullptr);
1031
1032 // Find minimum cost label for each node directly from buckets
1033 // without creating intermediate label maps
1034 for (auto &bucket : fw_buckets) {
1035 for (const Label *label : bucket.get_labels()) {
1036 const size_t node_id = label->node_id;
1037 if (!min_fw_labels[node_id] || label->cost < min_fw_labels[node_id]->cost) {
1038 min_fw_labels[node_id] = label;
1039 }
1040 }
1041 }
1042
1043 for (auto &bucket : bw_buckets) {
1044 for (const Label *label : bucket.get_labels()) {
1045 const size_t node_id = label->node_id;
1046 if (!min_bw_labels[node_id] || label->cost < min_bw_labels[node_id]->cost) {
1047 min_bw_labels[node_id] = label;
1048 }
1049 }
1050 }
1051
1052 // Pre-compute the index of the "time" resource.
1053 const size_t time_index =
1054 std::distance(options.resources.begin(), std::find(options.resources.begin(), options.resources.end(), "time"));
1055
1056 // Precompute lookup tables for common values
1057 std::vector<double> fw_time_resources(num_nodes, 0.0);
1058 std::vector<double> bw_time_resources(num_nodes, 0.0);
1059 std::vector<double> fw_costs(num_nodes, 0.0);
1060 std::vector<double> bw_costs(num_nodes, 0.0);
1061
1062 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
1063 if (min_fw_labels[i]) {
1064 fw_time_resources[i] = min_fw_labels[i]->resources[time_index];
1065 fw_costs[i] = min_fw_labels[i]->cost;
1066 }
1067 if (min_bw_labels[i]) {
1068 bw_time_resources[i] = min_bw_labels[i]->resources[time_index];
1069 bw_costs[i] = min_bw_labels[i]->cost;
1070 }
1071 }
1072
1073 // Cache node data to avoid repeated lookups
1074 std::vector<double> node_durations(num_nodes);
1075 std::vector<std::vector<double>> node_consumptions(num_nodes, std::vector<double>(options.resources.size()));
1076
1077 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
1078 const VRPNode &node = nodes[i];
1079 node_durations[i] = node.duration;
1080 for (size_t r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) { node_consumptions[i][r] = node.consumption[r]; }
1081 }
1082
1083 size_t num_fixes = 0;
1084 const double gap_threshold = gap; // Cache this value
1085
1086 // Build a list of candidate node pairs to check
1087 struct NodePair {
1088 size_t i;
1089 size_t j;
1090 };
1091
1092 std::vector<NodePair> candidate_pairs;
1093 candidate_pairs.reserve(num_nodes * num_nodes / 4); // Estimate: 25% of pairs will be candidates
1094
1095 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
1096 if (!min_fw_labels[i]) continue;
1097
1098 for (size_t j = 0; j < num_nodes; ++j) {
1099 if (i == j || !min_bw_labels[j]) continue;
1100 candidate_pairs.push_back({i, j});
1101 }
1102 }
1103
1104 // Process candidate pairs in batches to improve cache locality
1105 const size_t BATCH_SIZE = 256;
1106 std::vector<std::pair<size_t, size_t>> arcs_to_fix;
1107 arcs_to_fix.reserve(BATCH_SIZE);
1108
1109 for (size_t start = 0; start < candidate_pairs.size(); start += BATCH_SIZE) {
1110 const size_t end = std::min(start + BATCH_SIZE, candidate_pairs.size());
1111 arcs_to_fix.clear();
1112
1113 for (size_t idx = start; idx < end; ++idx) {
1114 const auto &pair = candidate_pairs[idx];
1115 const size_t i = pair.i;
1116 const size_t j = pair.j;
1117
1118 const double cost = getcij(min_fw_labels[i]->node_id, min_bw_labels[j]->node_id);
1119
1120 // Early rejection using precomputed time resources (often most
1121 // restrictive)
1122 if (options.resources[time_index] == "time") {
1123 if (fw_time_resources[i] + cost + node_durations[min_fw_labels[i]->node_id] > bw_time_resources[j]) {
1124 continue;
1125 }
1126 }
1127
1128 // Check all other resources
1129 bool violated = false;
1130 const size_t fw_node_id = min_fw_labels[i]->node_id;
1131
1132 for (size_t r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
1133 if (r != time_index) {
1134 if (min_fw_labels[i]->resources[r] + node_consumptions[fw_node_id][r] >
1135 min_bw_labels[j]->resources[r]) {
1136 violated = true;
1137 break;
1138 }
1139 }
1140 }
1141
1142 // If all resource constraints are satisfied and the cost exceeds
1143 // the gap, fix the arc
1144 if (!violated && (fw_costs[i] + cost + bw_costs[j] > gap_threshold)) {
1145 arcs_to_fix.emplace_back(i, j);
1146 ++num_fixes;
1147 }
1148 }
1149
1150 // Fix arcs in batch
1151 for (const auto &[i, j] : arcs_to_fix) { fix_arc(i, j); }
1152 }
1153
1154 if (S == Stage::Four) {
1155 fmt::print("Applied {} arc fixes\n", num_fixes);
1156 fmt::print("\033[34m_ARC FIXING PROCEDURE FINISHED\033[0m\n");
1157 }
1158}

Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ initInfo()

void BucketGraph::initInfo ( )
static

Prints the configuration information of the BucketGraph.

This function outputs the configuration details including resource size, number of clients, and maximum SRC cuts. It also conditionally prints whether RIH, RCC, and SRC are enabled or disabled based on the preprocessor directives.

The output format is as follows:

+-------------------------------—+ | CONFIGURATION INFO | +-------------------------------—+ Resources: <R_SIZE> Number of Clients: <N_SIZE> Maximum SRC cuts: <MAX_SRC_CUTS> RIH: <enabled/disabled> RCC: <enabled/disabled> SRC: <enabled/disabled> +-------------------------------—+

Definition at line 874 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

874 {
875 // Print header
876 fmt::print("\n+----------------------------------+\n");
877 fmt::print("| CONFIGURATION INFO |\n");
878 fmt::print("+----------------------------------+\n");
879
880 // Print Resource size
881 fmt::print("Resources: {}\n", R_SIZE);
882 fmt::print("Number of Clients: {}\n", N_SIZE - 2);
883
884 // Conditional configuration (RIH enabled/disabled)
885#ifdef RIH
886 fmt::print("RIH: enabled\n");
887#else
888 fmt::print("RIH: disabled\n");
889#endif
890#ifdef RCC
891 fmt::print("RCC: enabled\n");
892#else
893 fmt::print("RCC: disabled\n");
894#endif
895#ifdef SRC
896 fmt::print("SRC: enabled\n");
897#else
898 fmt::print("SRC: disabled\n");
899#endif
900 fmt::print("+----------------------------------+\n");
901
902 fmt::print("\n");
903}

bool BucketGraph::is_dominated ( const Label *__restrict new_label,

inlinenoexcept

Checks if a label is dominated by a new label based on cost and resource conditions.

Definition at line 1040 of file BucketSolve.h.

1040 {
1041 // A label cannot dominate if its cost is higher.
1042 // const double cost_diff = label->cost - new_label->cost;
1043 if (numericutils::gt(label->cost, new_label->cost)) { return false; }
1044
1045 // Compare resource vectors.
1046 const auto *__restrict new_res = new_label->resources.data();
1047 const auto *__restrict lbl_res = label->resources.data();
1048
1049 // Prefetch resource data
1050 __builtin_prefetch(new_res, 0,
1051 3); // 0 = read only, 3 = high temporal locality
1052 __builtin_prefetch(lbl_res, 0, 3);
1053
1054 const size_t n_res = options.resources.size();
1055 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
1056 // In the Forward direction, each resource value of 'label' must
1057 // not exceed that of 'new_label'
1058 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_res; ++i) {
1059 if (numericutils::gt(lbl_res[i], new_res[i])) { return false; }
1060 }
1061 } else if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward) {
1062 // In the Backward direction, each resource value of 'label'
1063 // must not be less than that of 'new_label'
1064 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_res; ++i) {
1065 if (numericutils::lt(lbl_res[i], new_res[i])) { return false; }
1066 }
1067 }
1068
1069 // visits of label.
1070 if constexpr (S == Stage::Three || S == Stage::Four || S == Stage::Enumerate) {
1071 const size_t n_bitmap = label->visited_bitmap.size();
1072
1073 // Prefetch bitmap data
1074 __builtin_prefetch(label->visited_bitmap.data(), 0, 3);
1075 __builtin_prefetch(new_label->visited_bitmap.data(), 0, 3);
1076
1077 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_bitmap; ++i) {
1078 // Every visited node in 'label' must also be visited in
1079 // 'new_label'.
1080 if ((label->visited_bitmap[i] & new_label->visited_bitmap[i]) != label->visited_bitmap[i]) { return false; }
1081 }
1082 }
1083#ifdef SRC
1084 // For Stage Four or Enumerate, apply additional SRC-based cost
1085 // adjustments.
1086 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four || S == Stage::Enumerate) {
1087 const double local_cost_diff = label->cost - new_label->cost;
1088 const auto *__restrict lbl_srcs = label->SRCmap.data();
1089 const auto *__restrict new_lbl_srcs = new_label->SRCmap.data();
1090
1091 // Prefetch SRCmap data
1092 __builtin_prefetch(lbl_srcs, 0, 3);
1093 __builtin_prefetch(new_lbl_srcs, 0, 3);
1094
1095 const auto &active_cuts = cut_storage->getActiveCuts();
1096 double dual_sum = 0.0;
1097 const size_t n = active_cuts.size();
1098 for (size_t i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
1099 const auto &cut = active_cuts[i];
1100 // Branch prediction hint: likely false.
1101 if (__builtin_expect(lbl_srcs[cut.index] > new_lbl_srcs[cut.index], 0)) {
1102 dual_sum += cut.dual_value;
1103 if (__builtin_expect((numericutils::gt(local_cost_diff, dual_sum)), 0)) { return false; }
1104 }
1105 }
1106 }
1107#endif
1108 return true;
1109}

◆ is_node_unreachable()

bool BucketGraph::is_node_unreachable ( const std::array< uint64_t, num_words > & bitmap,
int node_id )
inlinestatic

Checks if a node is unreachable based on a bitmap.

This function determines if a node, identified by its node_id, is unreachable by checking a specific bit in a bitmap. The bitmap is represented as an array of 64-bit unsigned integers.

577 }

◆ is_node_visited()

bool BucketGraph::is_node_visited ( const std::array< uint64_t, num_words > & bitmap,
int node_id )
inlinestatic

Checks if a node has been visited based on a bitmap.

This function determines if a specific node, identified by node_id, has been visited by checking the corresponding bit in a bitmap array. The bitmap is an array of 64-bit unsigned integers, where each bit represents the visited status of a node.

◆ labeling_algorithm()

template<Direction D, Stage S, Full F>
std::vector< double > BucketGraph::labeling_algorithm ( )

Performs the labeling algorithm on the BucketGraph.

Definition at line 203 of file BucketSolve.h.

203 {
204 // Cache bucket and auxiliary structure references to avoid repeated
205 // lookups.
206 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
207 const auto &topological_order = assign_buckets<D>(fw_topological_order, bw_topological_order);
208 const auto &sccs = assign_buckets<D>(fw_sccs, bw_sccs);
209 const auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
210 auto &c_bar = assign_buckets<D>(fw_c_bar, bw_c_bar);
211 auto &n_labels = assign_buckets<D>(n_fw_labels, n_bw_labels);
212 const auto &sorted_sccs = assign_buckets<D>(fw_sccs_sorted, bw_sccs_sorted);
213 const auto &n_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
214 auto &stat_n_labels = assign_buckets<D>(stat_n_labels_fw, stat_n_labels_bw);
215 auto &stat_n_dom = assign_buckets<D>(stat_n_dom_fw, stat_n_dom_bw);
216 auto &arc_scores = assign_buckets<D>(fw_arc_scores, bw_arc_scores);
217 auto &best_label = assign_buckets<D>(fw_best_label, bw_best_label);
218
219 // Reset the total label count.
220 n_labels = 0;
221
222 // Pre-calculate bitmap segments for dominance-checking.
223 const size_t n_segments = (n_buckets + 63) >> 6;
224 std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited(n_segments, 0);
225 std::vector<uint32_t> touched_segments;
226 touched_segments.reserve(std::min<size_t>(n_segments, 64));
227 std::vector<int> bucket_pos_in_scc(n_buckets, -1);
228
229 // Process only newly queued labels while preserving the bucket order
230 // inside each SCC.
231 std::vector<std::vector<Label *>> pending_labels_by_bucket;
232 std::vector<size_t> pending_label_cursor;
233 std::vector<uint8_t> bucket_is_active;
234 std::vector<int> active_bucket_heap;
235
236 // Precompute main resource index and q_star value for partial solutions.
237 constexpr bool is_partial = (F == Full::Partial);
238 int main_res_index = 0;
239 double q_star_value = 0.0;
240 if constexpr (is_partial) {
241 main_res_index = options.main_resources[0];
242 q_star_value = std::floor(q_star[main_res_index]);
243 }
244
245 // Reuse a thread-local vector for collecting labels to dominate.
246 static thread_local std::vector<Label *> tl_labels_to_dominate;
247 tl_labels_to_dominate.clear();
248 tl_labels_to_dominate.reserve(64); // Pre-reserve space to avoid reallocations
249
250 // Process each strongly connected component (SCC) in topological order.
251 for (const auto &scc_index : topological_order) {
252 const auto &scc_buckets = sorted_sccs[scc_index];
253 if (scc_buckets.empty()) continue;
254
255 pending_labels_by_bucket.clear();
256 pending_labels_by_bucket.resize(scc_buckets.size());
257 pending_label_cursor.assign(scc_buckets.size(), 0);
258 bucket_is_active.assign(scc_buckets.size(), 0);
259 active_bucket_heap.clear();
260
261 auto activate_bucket = [&](int bucket_pos) {
262 if (bucket_pos < 0 || bucket_is_active[bucket_pos]) return;
263 bucket_is_active[bucket_pos] = 1;
264 active_bucket_heap.push_back(bucket_pos);
265 std::push_heap(active_bucket_heap.begin(), active_bucket_heap.end(), std::greater<int>());
266 };
267
268 for (size_t bucket_idx = 0; bucket_idx < scc_buckets.size(); ++bucket_idx) {
269 const int bucket = scc_buckets[bucket_idx];
270 bucket_pos_in_scc[bucket] = static_cast<int>(bucket_idx);
271
272 const auto &bucket_labels = buckets[bucket].get_labels();
273 auto &pending_labels = pending_labels_by_bucket[bucket_idx];
274 pending_labels.reserve(bucket_labels.size());
275
276 for (Label *label : bucket_labels) {
277 if (!label->is_extended && !label->is_dominated) pending_labels.push_back(label);
278 }
279
280 if (!pending_labels.empty()) activate_bucket(static_cast<int>(bucket_idx));
281 }
282
283 while (!active_bucket_heap.empty()) {
284 std::pop_heap(active_bucket_heap.begin(), active_bucket_heap.end(), std::greater<int>());
285 const int bucket_idx = active_bucket_heap.back();
286 active_bucket_heap.pop_back();
287
288 const int bucket = scc_buckets[bucket_idx];
289 auto &pending_labels = pending_labels_by_bucket[bucket_idx];
290 size_t &pending_cursor = pending_label_cursor[bucket_idx];
291
292 while (pending_cursor < pending_labels.size()) {
293 Label *label = pending_labels[pending_cursor++];
294
295 // Skip labels already processed.
296 if (__builtin_expect(label->is_extended || label->is_dominated, 0)) continue;
297
298 // For partial solutions, check resource feasibility.
299 if constexpr (F != Full::PSTEP && F != Full::TSP) {
300 if constexpr (is_partial) {
301 const auto main_resource = label->resources[main_res_index];
302 if ((D == Direction::Forward && main_resource > q_star_value) ||
303 (D == Direction::Backward && main_resource <= q_star_value)) {
304 label->set_extended(true);
305 continue;
306 }
307 }
308 }
309
310 for (uint32_t segment_idx : touched_segments) { Bvisited[segment_idx] = 0; }
311 touched_segments.clear();
312
313 // Check if the label is dominated by labels in smaller
314 // buckets.
315 if constexpr (F != Full::TSP) {
316 if (DominatedInCompWiseSmallerBuckets<D, S>(label, bucket, c_bar, Bvisited, touched_segments,
317 stat_n_dom))
318 continue;
319 }
320
321 // Retrieve outgoing arcs for the current label.
322 const auto &node_arcs = buckets[bucket].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
323 if (__builtin_expect(node_arcs.empty(), 0)) {
324 label->set_extended(true);
325 continue;
326 }
327
328 // Prefetch first few arcs
329 if (!node_arcs.empty()) {
330 for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(size_t(4), node_arcs.size()); ++i) {
331 __builtin_prefetch(&node_arcs[i], 0, 3);
332 }
333 }
334
335 for (size_t arc_idx = 0; arc_idx < node_arcs.size(); ++arc_idx) {
336 const auto &arc = node_arcs[arc_idx];
337
338 // Prefetch next arc if available
339 if (arc_idx + 4 < node_arcs.size()) { __builtin_prefetch(&node_arcs[arc_idx + 4], 0, 3); }
340
341 auto new_label = Extend<D, S, ArcType::Bucket, Mutability::Mut, F>(label, arc);
342
343 // Process each new label produced by the extension.
344 if (new_label != nullptr) {
345 ++stat_n_labels;
346 const int to_bucket = new_label->vertex;
347 auto &mother_bucket = buckets[to_bucket];
348
349 // Prefetch the mother bucket and its labels
350 __builtin_prefetch(&mother_bucket, 0, 3);
351
352 const auto &to_bucket_labels = mother_bucket.get_labels();
353
354 // Prefetch first few destination bucket labels
355 if (!to_bucket_labels.empty()) {
356 for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(size_t(4), to_bucket_labels.size()); ++i) {
357 __builtin_prefetch(to_bucket_labels[i], 0, 3);
358 }
359 }
360
361 bool dominated = false;
362 if constexpr (S == Stage::One) {
363 // Stage One: mark higher-cost labels as
364 // dominated.
365 for (auto *existing_label : to_bucket_labels) {
366 if (existing_label->is_dominated) continue;
367 if (label->cost < existing_label->cost)
368 existing_label->set_dominated(true);
369 else {
370 dominated = true;
371 break;
372 }
373 }
374 } else {
375 // Later stages: use a SIMD-friendly, unrolled
376 // loop.
377 auto check_dominance_in_bucket = [&](std::span<Label *const> labels,
378 uint &stat_n_dom) -> bool {
379#ifdef __AVX2__
380 if (labels.size() >= AVX_LIM)
381 return check_dominance_against_vector<D, S>(
382 new_label, labels, cut_storage, stat_n_dom);
383 else
384#endif
385 {
386 const size_t size = labels.size();
387 size_t i = 0;
388
389 // Process one label at a time but with
390 // prefetching ahead
391 for (; i < size; ++i) {
392 // Prefetch several labels ahead for
393 // better memory access patterns
394 if (i + 8 < size) { __builtin_prefetch(labels[i + 8], 0, 3); }
395
396 // Process one label with branch
397 // prediction hint
398 Label *current_label = labels[i];
399 if (__builtin_expect(!current_label->is_dominated, 1)) {
400 // Cache access to current_label
401 // to avoid multiple
402 // dereferences
403 if (is_dominated<D, S>(new_label, current_label)) {
404 ++stat_n_dom;
405 return true;
406 }
407 }
408 }
409 return false;
410 }
411 };
412 dominated =
413 mother_bucket.check_dominance(new_label, check_dominance_in_bucket, stat_n_dom);
414 }
415
416 if (!dominated) {
417 // Reuse thread-local vector to avoid
418 // re-allocation.
419 tl_labels_to_dominate.clear();
420
421 const size_t to_bucket_size = to_bucket_labels.size();
422 size_t i = 0;
423
424 // Process dominance checks in blocks for better
425 // cache usage
426 for (; i + 3 < to_bucket_size; i += 4) {
427 // Prefetch ahead
428 if (i + 8 < to_bucket_size) {
429 __builtin_prefetch(to_bucket_labels[i + 4], 0, 3);
430 __builtin_prefetch(to_bucket_labels[i + 5], 0, 3);
431 __builtin_prefetch(to_bucket_labels[i + 6], 0, 3);
432 __builtin_prefetch(to_bucket_labels[i + 7], 0, 3);
433 }
434
435 auto *l0 = to_bucket_labels[i];
436 auto *l1 = to_bucket_labels[i + 1];
437 auto *l2 = to_bucket_labels[i + 2];
438 auto *l3 = to_bucket_labels[i + 3];
439
440 if (!l0->is_dominated && is_dominated<D, S>(l0, new_label))
441 tl_labels_to_dominate.push_back(l0);
442 if (!l1->is_dominated && is_dominated<D, S>(l1, new_label))
443 tl_labels_to_dominate.push_back(l1);
444 if (!l2->is_dominated && is_dominated<D, S>(l2, new_label))
445 tl_labels_to_dominate.push_back(l2);
446 if (!l3->is_dominated && is_dominated<D, S>(l3, new_label))
447 tl_labels_to_dominate.push_back(l3);
448 }
449
450 // Process remaining labels
451 for (; i < to_bucket_size; ++i) {
452 auto *existing_label = to_bucket_labels[i];
453 if (!existing_label->is_dominated && is_dominated<D, S>(existing_label, new_label))
454 tl_labels_to_dominate.push_back(existing_label);
455 }
456
457 // Mark dominated labels in batch
458 for (auto *existing_label : tl_labels_to_dominate) existing_label->set_dominated(true);
459
460 ++n_labels;
461#ifdef SORTED_LABELS
462 mother_bucket.add_sorted_label(new_label);
463#elif defined(LIMITED_BUCKETS)
464 mother_bucket.sorted_label(new_label, BUCKET_CAPACITY);
465#else
466 mother_bucket.add_label(new_label);
467#endif
468 const int to_bucket_pos = bucket_pos_in_scc[to_bucket];
469 if (to_bucket_pos != -1) {
470 pending_labels_by_bucket[to_bucket_pos].push_back(new_label);
471 activate_bucket(to_bucket_pos);
472 }
473 }
474 }
475 }
476 label->set_extended(true);
477 }
478
479 bucket_is_active[bucket_idx] = 0;
480 }
481
482 for (const int bucket : scc_buckets) { bucket_pos_in_scc[bucket] = -1; }
483
484 // Update c_bar values for all buckets in the current SCC.
485 auto sorted_buckets = sorted_sccs[scc_index];
486 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
487 pdqsort(sorted_buckets.begin(), sorted_buckets.end(),
488 [&](int a, int b) { return buckets[a].get_lb() < buckets[b].get_lb(); });
489 } else {
490 pdqsort(sorted_buckets.begin(), sorted_buckets.end(),
491 [&](int a, int b) { return buckets[a].get_ub() > buckets[b].get_ub(); });
492 }
493
494 // Prefetch data for c_bar updates
495 if (!sorted_buckets.empty()) {
496 for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(size_t(4), sorted_buckets.size()); ++i) {
497 __builtin_prefetch(&buckets[sorted_buckets[i]], 0, 3);
498 __builtin_prefetch(&Phi[sorted_buckets[i]], 0, 3);
499 }
500 }
501
502 // Batch processing of c_bar updates for improved cache locality.
503 for (size_t i = 0; i < sorted_buckets.size(); ++i) {
504 const int bucket = sorted_buckets[i];
505
506 // Prefetch data for next iterations
507 if (i + 4 < sorted_buckets.size()) {
508 __builtin_prefetch(&buckets[sorted_buckets[i + 4]], 0, 3);
509 __builtin_prefetch(&Phi[sorted_buckets[i + 4]], 0, 3);
510 }
511
512 double min_c_bar = buckets[bucket].get_cb();
513 const auto &phi_buckets = Phi[bucket];
514
515 // Prefetch first few phi buckets
516 if (!phi_buckets.empty()) {
517 for (size_t j = 0; j < std::min(size_t(4), phi_buckets.size()); ++j) {
518 __builtin_prefetch(&c_bar[phi_buckets[j]], 0, 3);
519 }
520 }
521
522 // Process phi buckets in blocks of 4 for better cache efficiency
523 size_t j = 0;
524 for (; j + 3 < phi_buckets.size(); j += 4) {
525 // Prefetch next block
526 if (j + 8 < phi_buckets.size()) {
527 __builtin_prefetch(&c_bar[phi_buckets[j + 4]], 0, 3);
528 __builtin_prefetch(&c_bar[phi_buckets[j + 5]], 0, 3);
529 __builtin_prefetch(&c_bar[phi_buckets[j + 6]], 0, 3);
530 __builtin_prefetch(&c_bar[phi_buckets[j + 7]], 0, 3);
531 }
532
533 // Compute min across 4 values at once
534 double val0 = c_bar[phi_buckets[j]];
535 double val1 = c_bar[phi_buckets[j + 1]];
536 double val2 = c_bar[phi_buckets[j + 2]];
537 double val3 = c_bar[phi_buckets[j + 3]];
538
539 min_c_bar = std::min(min_c_bar, std::min(std::min(val0, val1), std::min(val2, val3)));
540 }
541
542 // Process remaining phi buckets
543 for (; j < phi_buckets.size(); ++j) { min_c_bar = std::min(min_c_bar, c_bar[phi_buckets[j]]); }
544
545 c_bar[bucket] = min_c_bar;
546 }
547 }
548
549 // Store the best label from the labeling process.
550 if (Label *best_label_run = get_best_label<D>(topological_order, c_bar, sccs)) best_label = best_label_run;
551
552 return c_bar;
553}

◆ mono_initialization()

void BucketGraph::mono_initialization ( )

Initializes the BucketGraph for the mono-directional case.

Definition at line 593 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

593 {
594 // Clear previous data
595 merged_labels.clear();
596 merged_labels.reserve(100);
597 fw_c_bar.clear();
598 bw_c_bar.clear();
599
600 dominance_checks_per_bucket.assign(fw_buckets_size + 1, 0);
601 non_dominated_labels_per_bucket = 0;
602
603 // Resize cost vectors to match the number of buckets
604 fw_c_bar.resize(fw_buckets_size, std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
605 bw_c_bar.resize(bw_buckets_size, std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
606
607 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<Direction::Forward>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
608 auto &num_bucket_index = assign_buckets<Direction::Forward>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
609
610 int num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
611 std::vector<double> total_ranges(num_intervals);
612 std::vector<double> base_intervals(num_intervals);
613
614 auto &VRPNode = nodes[0]; // Example for the first node
615
616 // Calculate base intervals and total ranges for each resource dimension
617 for (int r = 0; r < intervals.size(); ++r) {
618 base_intervals[r] = (VRPNode.ub[r] - VRPNode.lb[r]) / intervals[r].interval;
619 }
620
621 // Clear forward and backward buckets
622 for (auto b = 0; b < fw_buckets.size(); b++) {
623 fw_buckets[b].clear();
624 bw_buckets[b].clear();
625 }
626
627 // Initialize forward buckets (generic for multiple dimensions)
628 std::vector<int> current_pos(num_intervals, 0);
629
630 std::vector<double> interval_starts(num_intervals);
631 std::vector<double> interval_ends(num_intervals);
632
633 // Initialize interval_starts to lower bounds and interval_ends to upper
634 // bounds
635 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
636 interval_starts[r] = VRPNode.lb[r];
637 interval_ends[r] = VRPNode.ub[r];
638 }
639 int offset_fw = 0;
640 int offset_bw = 0;
641
642 // Lambda to handle forward and backward direction initialization across
643 // combinations
644 auto initialize_intervals_combinations = [&](bool is_forward) {
645 int &offset = is_forward ? offset_fw : offset_bw;
646 auto &label_pool = is_forward ? label_pool_fw : label_pool_bw;
647 auto &buckets = is_forward ? fw_buckets : bw_buckets;
648 const auto &depot_id = is_forward ? options.depot : options.end_depot;
649 // print depot_id
650 int calculated_index_base =
651 is_forward ? num_buckets_index_fw[options.depot] : num_buckets_index_bw[options.end_depot];
652
653 std::vector<int> current_pos(num_intervals,
654 0); // Tracks current position in each interval dimension
655 std::vector<double> interval_bounds(num_intervals);
656
657 // Recursive lambda to generate all combinations of intervals
658 std::function<void(int)> generate_combinations = [&](int depth) {
659 if (depth == num_intervals) {
660 // All dimensions processed, now initialize for the current
661 // combination
662 auto depot = label_pool->acquire();
663 int calculated_index = calculated_index_base + offset;
664
665 // Set interval_bounds to current combination of interval
666 // starts or ends
667 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
668 interval_bounds[r] =
669 is_forward ? interval_starts[r] + current_pos[r] * roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(base_intervals[r])
670 : interval_ends[r] - current_pos[r] * roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(base_intervals[r]);
671 }
672
673 // print interval
674 // Initialize depot with the current interval boundaries
675 depot->initialize(calculated_index, 0.0, interval_bounds, depot_id);
676 depot->is_extended = false;
677 depot->cost += pstep_duals.getThreeTwoDualValue(depot_id);
678 depot->cost += pstep_duals.getThreeThreeDualValue(depot_id);
679 depot->addNode(depot_id);
680 set_node_visited(depot->visited_bitmap, depot_id);
681 SRC_MODE_BLOCK(depot->SRCmap.assign(cut_storage->SRCDuals.size(), 0);)
682 buckets[calculated_index].add_label(depot);
683 buckets[calculated_index].node_id = depot_id;
684
685 offset++; // Increment offset for each combination
686 return;
687 }
688
689 // Loop through each interval in the current dimension
690 for (int k = 0; k < intervals[depth].interval; ++k) {
691 current_pos[depth] = k;
692 generate_combinations(depth + 1);
693 }
694 };
695
696 // Start generating combinations from depth 0
697 generate_combinations(0);
698 };
699
700 // Call the lambda for both forward and backward directions, ensuring
701 // all combinations are processed
702 initialize_intervals_combinations(true); // Forward direction
703}
Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ ObtainJumpBucketArcs()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::ObtainJumpBucketArcs ( )

Obtains jump bucket arcs for the BucketGraph.

This function iterates over each bucket in the BucketGraph and adds jump arcs to the set of buckets. It checks if each arc exists in the given Gamma vector for the current bucket. If an arc exists, it adds the corresponding bucket to the set of buckets to add jump arcs to. It then removes the non-component-wise minimal buckets from the set. Finally, it adds jump arcs from the current bucket to each bucket in the set.

Definition at line 355 of file BucketJump.h.

355 {
356 // Select direction-specific containers.
357 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
358 auto &num_buckets_idx = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
359 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
360 auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
361 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
362
363 int arc_counter = 0; // Count of jump arcs added
364 int missing_counter = 0; // Count of missing paths
365
366 // Clear jump arcs for all nodes.
367 for (auto &node : nodes) { node.clear_jump_arcs<D>(); }
368
369 // Process each bucket.
370 for (int b = 0; b < buckets_size; ++b) {
371 std::vector<int> B_bar; // Temporary storage for valid adjacent
372 // bucket indices
373
374 const int current_node_id = buckets[b].node_id;
375 const auto &bucket_arcs = buckets[b].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
376 const auto &orig_arcs = nodes[current_node_id].template get_arcs<D>();
377
378 // Skip if there are no bucket arcs.
379 if (bucket_arcs.empty()) { continue; }
380
381 // For each original arc from the current node...
382 for (const auto &orig_arc : orig_arcs) {
383 const int from_node = orig_arc.from;
384 const int to_node = orig_arc.to;
385 bool have_path = false;
386
387 // Check if the current bucket arcs contain the desired
388 // path.
389 for (const auto &gamma : bucket_arcs) {
390 if (is_bucket_fixed<D>(gamma.from_bucket, gamma.to_bucket)) { continue; }
391
392 const int from_node_b = buckets[gamma.from_bucket].node_id;
393 const int to_node_b = buckets[gamma.to_bucket].node_id;
394
395 if (from_node == from_node_b && to_node == to_node_b) {
396 have_path = true;
397 break; // Path found: no need to search further.
398 }
399 }
400
401 // If no valid path was found, try to obtain a jump arc.
402 if (!have_path) {
403 ++missing_counter;
404
405 // Determine bucket range for the current node.
406 const int start_bucket = num_buckets_idx[current_node_id];
407 const int node_buckets = num_buckets[current_node_id];
408
409 // Lambda to process candidate adjacent buckets.
410 auto process_bucket = [&](int current_b, int candidate_b) {
411 // Only proceed if candidate bucket is adjacent (per
412 // Phi).
413 const auto &phi_candidate = Phi[candidate_b];
414 if (std::find(phi_candidate.begin(), phi_candidate.end(), current_b) == phi_candidate.end()) return;
415
416 // Check candidate bucket's arcs.
417 const auto &candidate_arcs = buckets[candidate_b].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
418 for (const auto &gamma_candidate : candidate_arcs) {
419 if (is_bucket_fixed<D>(gamma_candidate.from_bucket, gamma_candidate.to_bucket)) continue;
420
421 const int from_node_candidate = buckets[gamma_candidate.from_bucket].node_id;
422 const int to_node_candidate = buckets[gamma_candidate.to_bucket].node_id;
423
424 // If the candidate arc matches the original
425 // arc...
426 if (from_node == from_node_candidate && to_node == to_node_candidate) {
427 B_bar.push_back(candidate_b);
428 std::vector<double> res = gamma_candidate.resource_increment;
429
430 const double cost = gamma_candidate.cost_increment;
431
432 // Add jump arcs both in the bucket and at
433 // the node level.
434 buckets[current_b].template add_jump_arc<D>(current_b, candidate_b, res, cost);
435 nodes[buckets[current_b].node_id].template add_jump_arc<D>(current_b, candidate_b, res,
436 cost, to_node);
437 buckets[current_b].template add_bucket_arc<D>(current_b, candidate_b, res, cost, true);
438 ++arc_counter;
439 }
440 }
441 };
442
443 // Process successor candidates within the same node block.
444 // A valid candidate must still satisfy Phi-adjacency inside
445 // process_bucket.
446 for (int candidate_b = b + 1; candidate_b < start_bucket + node_buckets; ++candidate_b) {
447 process_bucket(b, candidate_b);
448 }
449 }
450 }
451 }
452
453 // Print summary of jump arcs added.
454 CONDITIONAL(D, print_info("[Fw] added {} jump arcs\n", arc_counter),
455 print_info("[Bw] added {} jump arcs\n", arc_counter));
456}
CONDITIONAL
#define CONDITIONAL(D, FW_ACTION, BW_ACTION)
Definition Definitions.h:380
Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ print_statistics()

void BucketGraph::print_statistics ( )

Prints the statistics of the bucket graph.

This function outputs a formatted table displaying various metrics related to the bucket graph. The table includes headers and values for forward and backward labels, as well as dominance checks. The output is color-coded for better readability:

  • Bold blue for metric names
  • Green for values (backward labels)
  • Reset color to default after each line

The table structure: +-------------------—+--------------—+--------------—+ | Metric | Forward | Backward | +-------------------—+--------------—+--------------—+ | Labels | <stat_n_labels_fw> | <stat_n_labels_bw> | | Dominance Check | <stat_n_dom_fw> | <stat_n_dom_bw> | +-------------------—+--------------—+--------------—+

Note: The actual values for the metrics (e.g., stat_n_labels_fw, stat_n_labels_bw, etc.) should be provided by the corresponding member variables or functions.

Definition at line 739 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

739 {
740 const char *blue_bold = "\033[1;34m"; // Bold blue for metrics
741 const char *green = "\033[32m"; // Green for values (backward labels)
742 const char *reset = "\033[0m"; // Reset color
743
744 // Print table header with horizontal line and separators
745 fmt::print("\n+--------------------------------------------------------+\n");
746 fmt::print("|{}{:<20}{}| {:<15} | {:<15} |\n", blue_bold, " Metric", reset, " Forward", " Backward");
747 fmt::print("+--------------------------------------------------------+\n");
748
749 // Print labels for forward and backward with bold blue metric
750 fmt::print("|{}{:<20}{}| {:<15} | {:<15} |\n", blue_bold, " Labels", reset, stat_n_labels_fw, stat_n_labels_bw);
751
752 // Print dominated forward and backward labels with bold blue metric
753 fmt::print("|{}{:<20}{}| {:<15} | {:<15} |\n", blue_bold, " Dominance Check", reset, stat_n_dom_fw, stat_n_dom_bw);
754
755 // Print the final horizontal line
756 fmt::print("+--------------------------------------------------------+\n");
757
758 fmt::print("\n");
759}
reset
constexpr auto reset
Definition Definitions.h:140

◆ process_all_resources()

template<Direction D, typename Gamma, typename VRPNode>
bool BucketGraph::process_all_resources ( std::vector< double > & new_resources,
const std::array< double, R_SIZE > & initial_resources,
const Gamma & gamma,
const VRPNode & theNode,
size_t N )

Processes all resources by iterating through them and applying constraints.

This function recursively processes each resource in the new_resources vector by calling process_resource for each index from I to N-1. If any resource processing fails (i.e., process_resource returns false), the function returns false immediately. If all resources are processed successfully, the function returns true.

Definition at line 108 of file BucketRes.h.

110 {
111 for (size_t I = 0; I < N; ++I) {
112 switch (static_cast<ResourceType>(options.resource_type[I])) {
113 [[likely]] case ResourceType::Disposable:
114 if (!process_disposable_resource<D>(new_resources[I], initial_resources[I], gamma.resource_increment[I],
115 theNode.lb[I], theNode.ub[I])) {
116 return false;
117 }
118 break;
119
120 case ResourceType::NonDisposable:
121 if (!process_non_disposable_resource<D>(new_resources[I], initial_resources[I], gamma.resource_increment[I],
122 theNode.lb[I], theNode.ub[I])) {
123 return false;
124 }
125 break;
126
127 case ResourceType::Binary: new_resources[I] = process_binary_resource<D>(gamma.resource_increment[I]); break;
128
129 case ResourceType::Battery:
130 if (!process_battery_resource<D>(new_resources[I], initial_resources[I], theNode.is_station,
131 options.battery_capacity)) {
132 return false;
133 }
134 break;
135
136 case ResourceType::RechargeCount:
137 if (!process_recharge_count<D>(new_resources[I], initial_resources[I], theNode.is_station,
138 options.max_recharges)) {
139 return false;
140 }
141 break;
142
143 case ResourceType::RechargeTime:
144 if (!process_recharge_time<D>(new_resources[I], initial_resources[I], theNode.is_station,
145 options.max_recharge_time)) {
146 return false;
147 }
148 break;
149
150 case ResourceType::MTW:
151 if (!process_mtw_resource<D>(new_resources[I], initial_resources[I], gamma.resource_increment[I],
152 theNode)) {
153 return false;
154 }
155 break;
156 }
157 }
158 return true;

◆ process_resource()

template<Direction D, typename Gamma, typename VRPNode>
bool BucketGraph::process_resource ( double & new_resource,
const std::array< double, R_SIZE > & initial_resources,
const Gamma & gamma,
const VRPNode & theNode,
size_t I )
constexpr

Processes a resource based on its disposability type and direction.

This function updates the new_resource value based on the initial resources, the increment provided by gamma, and the constraints defined by theNode. The behavior varies depending on the disposability type of the resource.

void BucketGraph::prune_ng_cycles ( int max_age,
int min_usage,
int current_iteration )

Definition at line 279 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

279 {
280 for (auto it = ng_cycles.begin(); it != ng_cycles.end();) {
281 if (current_iteration - it->last_used_iteration > max_age && it->usage_count < min_usage) {
282 // Remove the corresponding forbidden edges from the
283 // neighborhoods_bitmap.
284 for (size_t i = 0; i < it->cycle.size() - 1; ++i) {
285 int v1 = it->cycle[i];
286 int v2 = it->cycle[i + 1];
287 // Clear the bit corresponding to v2 in the neighborhood of
288 // v1.
289 size_t segment = static_cast<size_t>(v2) >> 6;
290 size_t bit_position = static_cast<size_t>(v2) & 63;
291 neighborhoods_bitmap[v1][segment] &= ~bit_mask_lookup[bit_position];
292
293 // If your approach was aggressive, also clear the reverse.
294 segment = static_cast<size_t>(v1) >> 6;
295 bit_position = static_cast<size_t>(v1) & 63;
296 neighborhoods_bitmap[v2][segment] &= ~bit_mask_lookup[bit_position];
297 }
298 it = ng_cycles.erase(it);
299 } else {
300 ++it;
301 }
302 }
303}

◆ redefine()

void BucketGraph::redefine ( int bucketInterval)
inline

Redefines the bucket intervals and reinitializes various data structures.

This function updates the bucket interval and reinitializes the intervals, buckets, fixed arcs, and fixed buckets. It also generates arcs and sorts them for each node.

◆ reset_fixed()

void BucketGraph::reset_fixed ( )
inline

Resets all fixed arcs in the graph.

This function iterates over each row in the fixed_arcs matrix and sets all elements to 0. It effectively clears any fixed arc constraints that may have been previously set.

◆ reset_pool()

void BucketGraph::reset_pool ( )
inline

Resets the forward and backward label pools.

This function resets both the forward (label_pool_fw) and backward (label_pool_bw) label pools to their initial states. It is typically used to clear any existing labels and prepare the pools for reuse.

◆ run_labeling_algorithms()

template<Stage state, Full fullness>
void BucketGraph::run_labeling_algorithms ( std::vector< double > & forward_cbar,
std::vector< double > & backward_cbar )

Runs forward and backward labeling algorithms in parallel and synchronizes the results.

T#his function creates tasks for forward and backward labeling algorithms using the provided scheduling mechanism. The tasks are executed in parallel, and the results are synchronized and stored in the provided vectors.

Definition at line 1248 of file BucketSolve.h.

1248 {
1249 // Schedule the forward labeling algorithm task on the scheduler.
1250 auto forward_task = stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) |
1251 stdexec::then([&]() { return labeling_algorithm<Direction::Forward, state, fullness>(); });
1252
1253 // Schedule the backward labeling algorithm task on the scheduler.
1254 auto backward_task = stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) |
1255 stdexec::then([&]() { return labeling_algorithm<Direction::Backward, state, fullness>(); });
1256
1257 // Combine the forward and backward tasks.
1258 auto combined_work = stdexec::when_all(std::move(forward_task), std::move(backward_task)) |
1259 stdexec::then([&](auto forward_result, auto backward_result) {
1260 // Store the results into the provided vectors.
1261 forward_cbar = std::move(forward_result);
1262 backward_cbar = std::move(backward_result);
1263 });
1264
1265 // Wait for all tasks to complete.
1266 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(combined_work));
1267}
Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ SCC_handler()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::SCC_handler ( )

Handles the computation of Strongly Connected Components (SCCs) for the BucketGraph.

This function processes the bucket graph to identify SCCs using Tarjan's algorithm. It extends the bucket graph with arcs defined by the Phi sets, computes the SCCs, and orders them topologically. It also sorts the buckets within each SCC based on their lower or upper bounds, depending on the direction. Finally, it splits the arcs for each SCC and removes duplicates.

Definition at line 745 of file BucketUtils.h.

745 {
746 // Get necessary references.
747 auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
748 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
749 auto &bucket_graph = assign_buckets<D>(fw_bucket_graph, bw_bucket_graph);
750
751 // Build an extended bucket graph from bucket_graph using Phi.
752 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<int, std::vector<int>> extended_bucket_graph = bucket_graph;
753 for (size_t i = 0; i < buckets.size(); ++i) {
754 extended_bucket_graph[static_cast<int>(i)];
755 const auto &phi_set = Phi[i];
756 if (phi_set.empty()) continue;
757 for (auto phi_bucket : phi_set) { extended_bucket_graph[phi_bucket].push_back(static_cast<int>(i)); }
758 }
759
760 // Compute SCCs using Tarjan's algorithm.
761 SCC scc_finder;
762 scc_finder.convertFromUnorderedMap(extended_bucket_graph);
763 auto sccs = scc_finder.tarjanSCC();
764 auto topological_order = scc_finder.topologicalOrderOfSCCs(sccs);
765
766#ifdef VERBOSE
767 // Optional: Print SCCs.
768 constexpr auto blue = "\033[34m";
769 constexpr auto reset = "\033[0m";
770 fmt::print((D == Direction::Forward) ? "FW SCCs:\n" : "BW SCCs:\n");
771 for (auto scc : topological_order) {
772 fmt::print("{}({}) -> {}", blue, scc, reset);
773 for (auto bucket : sccs[scc]) { fmt::print("{} ", bucket); }
774 fmt::print("\n");
775 }
776 fmt::print("\n");
777#endif
778
779 // Order SCCs according to the topological order.
780 std::vector<std::vector<int>> ordered_sccs;
781 ordered_sccs.reserve(sccs.size());
782 for (int scc : topological_order) { ordered_sccs.push_back(sccs[scc]); }
783
784 // Sort each SCC: For Forward, sort by bucket lower bound; for Backward,
785 // sort by bucket upper bound.
786 auto sorted_sccs = sccs; // Make a copy
787 for (auto &scc : sorted_sccs) {
788 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
789 pdqsort(scc.begin(), scc.end(), [&buckets](int a, int b) { return buckets[a].lb[0] < buckets[b].lb[0]; });
790 } else {
791 pdqsort(scc.begin(), scc.end(), [&buckets](int a, int b) { return buckets[a].ub[0] > buckets[b].ub[0]; });
792 }
793 }
794
795 // Ensure each VRP node has the appropriate SCC arc container allocated.
796 for (auto &node : nodes) {
797 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
798 node.fw_arcs_scc.resize(sccs.size());
799 } else {
800 node.bw_arcs_scc.resize(sccs.size());
801 }
802 }
803
804 // Lambda: Filter and collect arcs for a given bucket, node, and
805 // direction.
806 auto process_bucket_arcs = [&](int bucket, int scc_index) {
807 int from_node_id = buckets[bucket].node_id;
808 VRPNode &node = nodes[from_node_id];
809 // Select the proper arc container and node arcs based on the
810 // direction.
811 const auto &node_arcs = (D == Direction::Forward) ? node.fw_arcs : node.bw_arcs;
812 std::vector<Arc> &filtered_arcs =
813 (D == Direction::Forward) ? node.fw_arcs_scc[scc_index] : node.bw_arcs_scc[scc_index];
814
815 const auto &bucket_arcs = buckets[bucket].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
816 for (const auto &arc : bucket_arcs) {
817 int to_node_id = buckets[arc.to_bucket].node_id;
818 // Look for an arc with destination equal to to_node_id.
819 auto it = std::find_if(node_arcs.begin(), node_arcs.end(),
820 [&to_node_id](const Arc &a) { return a.to == to_node_id; });
821 if (it != node_arcs.end()) { filtered_arcs.push_back(*it); }
822 }
823 };
824
825 // For each SCC, process arcs for each bucket in the SCC.
826 int scc_ctr = 0;
827 for (const auto &scc : sccs) {
828 for (int bucket : scc) { process_bucket_arcs(bucket, scc_ctr); }
829 ++scc_ctr;
830 }
831
832 // Lambda: Sort and deduplicate arcs within each node's SCC arc
833 // container.
834 auto sortAndDedup = [](std::vector<Arc> &arcs) {
835 pdqsort(arcs.begin(), arcs.end(),
836 [](const Arc &a, const Arc &b) { return std::tie(a.from, a.to) < std::tie(b.from, b.to); });
837 auto last = std::unique(arcs.begin(), arcs.end(),
838 [](const Arc &a, const Arc &b) { return a.from == b.from && a.to == b.to; });
839 arcs.erase(last, arcs.end());
840 };
841
842 // For each node, sort and deduplicate all arc lists.
843 for (auto &node : nodes) {
844 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
845 for (auto &arcs : node.fw_arcs_scc) { sortAndDedup(arcs); }
846 } else {
847 for (auto &arcs : node.bw_arcs_scc) { sortAndDedup(arcs); }
848 }
849 }
850
851 std::vector<int> bucket_scc_rank(buckets.size(), -1);
852 for (size_t rank = 0; rank < ordered_sccs.size(); ++rank) {
853 for (int bucket : ordered_sccs[rank]) { bucket_scc_rank[bucket] = static_cast<int>(rank); }
854 }
855
856 // Build union-find structure for SCCs using the ordered SCCs.
857 UnionFind uf(ordered_sccs);
858 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
859 fw_ordered_sccs = ordered_sccs;
860 fw_topological_order = topological_order;
861 fw_sccs = sccs;
862 fw_sccs_sorted = sorted_sccs;
863 fw_bucket_scc_rank = std::move(bucket_scc_rank);
864 fw_union_find = uf;
865 } else {
866 bw_ordered_sccs = ordered_sccs;
867 bw_topological_order = topological_order;
868 bw_sccs = sccs;
869 bw_sccs_sorted = sorted_sccs;
870 bw_bucket_scc_rank = std::move(bucket_scc_rank);
871 bw_union_find = uf;
872 }
873}

◆ set_adjacency_list()

template<Symmetry SYM>
void BucketGraph::set_adjacency_list ( )

Definition at line 1161 of file BucketUtils.h.

1161 {
1162 // === Step 1: Clear Existing Arcs from Each Node ===
1163 for (auto &node : nodes) { node.clear_arcs(); }
1164
1165 // === Step 2: MST-Based Clustering ===
1166 MST mst_solver(nodes, [this](int from, int to) { return getcij(from, to); });
1167 auto mst = mst_solver.compute_mst();
1168
1169 // Collect and sort MST edge weights.
1170 std::vector<double> edge_weights;
1171 edge_weights.reserve(mst.size());
1172 for (const auto &[weight, from, to] : mst) { edge_weights.push_back(weight); }
1173 pdqsort(edge_weights.begin(), edge_weights.end());
1174
1175 // Compute theta as the 90th percentile (scaled down by 100).
1176 size_t p90_index = static_cast<size_t>(0.9 * edge_weights.size());
1177 double theta = (edge_weights[p90_index]) / 100.0;
1178 print_info("Computed theta: {}\n", theta);
1179
1180 // Cluster nodes and build mapping: job_id -> cluster_id.
1181 auto clusters = mst_solver.cluster(theta);
1182 print_info("Number of clusters: {}\n", clusters.size());
1183 std::vector<int> job_to_cluster(nodes.size(), -1);
1184 for (int cluster_id = 0; cluster_id < clusters.size(); ++cluster_id) {
1185 for (int job : clusters[cluster_id]) { job_to_cluster[job] = cluster_id; }
1186 }
1187
1188 // === Step 3: Precompute Resource Information ===
1189 // Find the index for the "time" resource.
1190 auto time_it = std::find(options.resources.begin(), options.resources.end(), "time");
1191 const int time_resource_idx =
1192 (time_it != options.resources.end()) ? std::distance(options.resources.begin(), time_it) : -1;
1193
1194 // === Step 4: Process Arcs for Each Node ===
1195
1196 // Helper lambda to compute resource increments.
1197 // Depending on symmetry, consumption is either taken as-is or averaged.
1198 auto compute_resource_increments = [&](const VRPNode &from_node, const VRPNode &to_node, double travel_cost,
1199 std::vector<double> &inc) {
1200 const int nres = options.resources.size();
1201 if constexpr (SYM == Symmetry::Asymmetric) {
1202 for (int r = 0; r < nres; ++r) {
1203 inc[r] = (r == time_resource_idx) ? travel_cost + from_node.duration : from_node.consumption[r];
1204 }
1205 } else {
1206 for (int r = 0; r < nres; ++r) {
1207 inc[r] = (r == time_resource_idx) ? (from_node.duration / 2.0 + travel_cost + to_node.duration / 2.0)
1208 : ((from_node.consumption[r] + to_node.consumption[r]) / 2.0);
1209 }
1210 }
1211 };
1212
1213 // Lambda to add forward and reverse arcs from a given node.
1214 auto add_arcs_for_node = [&](const VRPNode &curr_node) {
1215 // Containers to accumulate arcs before insertion.
1216 using ArcTuple = std::tuple<double, int, std::vector<double>, double>;
1217 std::vector<ArcTuple> forward_arcs;
1218 std::vector<ArcTuple> reverse_arcs;
1219 forward_arcs.reserve(nodes.size());
1220 reverse_arcs.reserve(nodes.size());
1221
1222 // Iterate over candidate next nodes.
1223 for (const auto &next_node : nodes) {
1224 // Skip if the candidate is a depot, the same node, or a pstep
1225 // depot.
1226 if (next_node.id == options.depot || curr_node.id == next_node.id ||
1227 (options.pstep && (next_node.id == options.pstep_depot || next_node.id == options.pstep_end_depot))) {
1228 continue;
1229 }
1230
1231 double travel_cost = getcij(curr_node.id, next_node.id);
1232 double cost_inc = travel_cost - next_node.cost;
1233
1234 // Skip self-loops (or if bucket indexes match; here using node
1235 // id as bucket id).
1236 if (curr_node.id == next_node.id) continue;
1237
1238 // Compute resource increments.
1239 std::vector<double> res_inc(options.resources.size());
1240 compute_resource_increments(curr_node, next_node, travel_cost, res_inc);
1241
1242 // Check feasibility: ensure resource consumption does not
1243 // exceed next node's upper bound.
1244 bool feasible = true;
1245 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
1246 if (numericutils::gt(curr_node.lb[r] + res_inc[r], next_node.ub[r])) {
1247 feasible = false;
1248 break;
1249 }
1250 }
1251 if (!feasible) continue;
1252
1253 // Compute priorities based on cluster membership.
1254 bool same_cluster = (job_to_cluster[curr_node.id] == job_to_cluster[next_node.id]);
1255 double forward_priority = (same_cluster ? 5.0 : 1.0) + 1.E-5 * next_node.start_time;
1256 double reverse_priority = (same_cluster ? 1.0 : 5.0) + 1.E-5 * curr_node.start_time;
1257
1258 // Collect arcs for forward and reverse directions.
1259 forward_arcs.emplace_back(forward_priority, next_node.id, res_inc, cost_inc);
1260 reverse_arcs.emplace_back(reverse_priority, next_node.id, res_inc, cost_inc);
1261 }
1262
1263 // Insert the arcs into the corresponding nodes.
1264 for (const auto &[priority, to_node, res_inc, cost_inc] : forward_arcs) {
1265 nodes[curr_node.id].template add_arc<Direction::Forward>(curr_node.id, to_node, res_inc, cost_inc,
1266 priority);
1267 }
1268 for (const auto &[priority, from_node, res_inc, cost_inc] : reverse_arcs) {
1269 nodes[from_node].template add_arc<Direction::Backward>(from_node, curr_node.id, res_inc, cost_inc,
1270 priority);
1271 }
1272 };
1273
1274 // Process each node, skipping the end depot.
1275 for (const auto &node : nodes) {
1276 if (node.id != options.end_depot) { add_arcs_for_node(node); }
1277 }
1278}
Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ set_adjacency_list_manual()

void BucketGraph::set_adjacency_list_manual ( )

Definition at line 479 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

479 {
480 // Clear existing arcs for each node
481 for (auto &node : nodes) {
482 node.clear_arcs(); // Remove any existing arcs associated with the
483 // node
484 }
485
486 // Step 1: Compute the clusters using MST-based clustering
487 MST mst_solver(nodes, [&](int from, int to) { return this->getcij(from, to); });
488 double theta = 1.0; // Experiment with different values of θ
489 auto clusters = mst_solver.cluster(theta);
490
491 // Create a job-to-cluster mapping (cluster ID for each job/node)
492 std::vector<int> job_to_cluster(nodes.size(), -1); // Mapping from job (node) to cluster ID
493 for (int cluster_id = 0; cluster_id < clusters.size(); ++cluster_id) {
494 for (int job : clusters[cluster_id]) { job_to_cluster[job] = cluster_id; }
495 }
496
497 // Step 2: Modify add_arcs_for_node to give priority based on cluster
498 // membership
499 auto add_arcs_for_node = [&](const VRPNode &node, int from_bucket, std::vector<double> &res_inc) {
500 using Arc = std::tuple<double, int, std::vector<double>,
501 double>; // Arc: priority, to_node, resource
502 // increments, cost increment
503
504 std::vector<Arc> best_arcs;
505 best_arcs.reserve(nodes.size()); // Reserve space for forward arcs
506
507 std::vector<Arc> best_arcs_rev;
508 best_arcs_rev.reserve(nodes.size()); // Reserve space for reverse arcs
509
510 for (const auto &next_node : nodes) {
511 if (!manual_arcs.has_arc(node.id, next_node.id)) continue; // Skip arcs not in the manual list
512
513 if (next_node.id == options.depot || node.id == next_node.id) continue; // Skip depot and same node
514
515 auto travel_cost = getcij(node.id, next_node.id); // Calculate travel cost
516 double cost_inc = travel_cost - next_node.cost; // Adjust cost increment by
517 // subtracting next node's cost
518
519 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
520 if (options.resources[r] == "time") {
521 res_inc[r] = travel_cost + node.duration; // Update resource increment
522 // based on node duration
523 } else {
524 res_inc[r] = node.consumption[r];
525 }
526 }
527 // res_inc[TIME_INDEX] += travel_cost; // Add travel time to
528 // resource increment
529
530 int to_bucket = next_node.id;
531 if (from_bucket == to_bucket) continue; // Skip arcs that loop back to the same bucket
532
533 bool feasible = true; // Check feasibility based on resource constraints
534 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
535 if (node.lb[r] + res_inc[r] > next_node.ub[r]) {
536 feasible = false;
537 break;
538 }
539 }
540 if (!feasible) continue; // Skip infeasible arcs
541
542 // Step 3: Calculate priority based on cluster membership
543 double priority_value;
544 double reverse_priority_value;
545 if (job_to_cluster[node.id] == job_to_cluster[next_node.id]) {
546 // Higher priority if both nodes are in the same cluster
547 priority_value = 5.0 + 1.E-5 * next_node.start_time; // Adjust weight for
548 // same-cluster priority
549 reverse_priority_value = 1.0 + 1.E-5 * node.start_time; // Adjust weight for
550 // same-cluster priority
551 } else {
552 // Lower priority for cross-cluster arcs
553 priority_value = 1.0 + 1.E-5 * next_node.start_time; // Higher base value for
554 // cross-cluster arcs
555 reverse_priority_value = 5.0 + 1.E-5 * node.start_time; // Higher base value for
556 // cross-cluster arcs
557 }
558
559 best_arcs.emplace_back(priority_value, next_node.id, res_inc,
560 cost_inc); // Store the forward arc
561 best_arcs_rev.emplace_back(reverse_priority_value, next_node.id, res_inc,
562 cost_inc); // Store the reverse arc
563 }
564
565 // Add forward arcs from the current node to its neighbors
566 for (const auto &arc : best_arcs) {
567 auto [priority_value, to_bucket, res_inc_local, cost_inc] = arc;
568 nodes[node.id].template add_arc<Direction::Forward>(node.id, to_bucket, res_inc_local, cost_inc,
569 priority_value); // Add forward arc
570 }
571
572 // Add reverse arcs from neighboring nodes to the current node
573 for (const auto &arc : best_arcs_rev) {
574 auto [priority_value, to_bucket, res_inc_local, cost_inc] = arc;
575 nodes[to_bucket].template add_arc<Direction::Backward>(to_bucket, node.id, res_inc_local, cost_inc,
576 priority_value); // Add reverse arc
577 }
578 };
579
580 // Step 4: Iterate over all nodes to set the adjacency list
581 for (const auto &VRPNode : nodes) {
582 if (VRPNode.id == options.end_depot) continue; // Skip the last node (depot)
583
584 std::vector<double> res_inc(intervals.size()); // Resource increment vector
585 add_arcs_for_node(VRPNode, VRPNode.id,
586 res_inc); // Add arcs for the current node
587 }
588}

void BucketGraph::set_node_unreachable ( std::array< uint64_t, num_words > & bitmap,
int node_id )
inlinestatic

Marks a node as unreachable in the given bitmap.

This function sets the bit corresponding to the specified node_id in the bitmap, indicating that the node is unreachable.

587 {
588 int word_index = node_id >> 6; // Determine which 64-bit segment
589 // contains the node_id

591 bitmap[word_index] |= (1ULL << bit_position);
592 }

◆ shallUpdateStep()

bool BucketGraph::shallUpdateStep ( )
inline

Definition at line 1243 of file BucketGraph.h.

1243 {
1244 int update_ctr = 0;
1245 ankerl::unordered_dense::set<int> updated_buckets;
1246
1247 // Iterate over all buckets and check if either forward or backward
1248 // bucket indicates a split is needed.
1249 for (size_t b = 0; b < fw_buckets.size(); ++b) {
1250 if (fw_buckets[b].shall_split || bw_buckets[b].shall_split) {
1251 const int inner_id = fw_buckets[b].node_id;
1252 // Skip depot buckets.
1253 if (inner_id == options.depot || inner_id == options.end_depot) { continue; }
1254 // If this node was already updated, skip it.
1255 if (updated_buckets.contains(inner_id)) { continue; }
1256 for (auto r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
1257 // If the bucket split value is already at maximum (100),
1258 // skip.(127)
1259 //
1260 if (fw_bucket_splits[inner_id][r] == 100) { continue; }
1261 // Update the bucket splits by doubling the current value,
1262 // capped at 100.
1263 const int update_target = std::min(fw_bucket_splits[inner_id][r] * 2, 100);
1264 fw_bucket_splits[inner_id][r] = update_target;
1265 bw_bucket_splits[inner_id][r] = update_target;
1266 updated_buckets.insert(inner_id);
1267 }
1268 update_ctr++;
1269 }
1270 }
1271
1272 // If we have reached a significant number of updates and we haven't
1273 // accumulated too many cuts, then update bucket splits.
1274 const int CHANGE_THRESHOLD = nodes.size() / 6;
1275 if (update_ctr >= CHANGE_THRESHOLD && cut_storage->size() < 40) {
1276 print_info("Updating bucket splits with {} changes\n", update_ctr);
1277
1278 // Redefine buckets in parallel for both forward and backward
1279 // directions.
1280 PARALLEL_SECTIONS(
1281 work, bi_sched,
1282 SECTION {
1283 // Forward bucket redefinition.
1284 define_buckets<Direction::Forward>();
1285 },
1286 SECTION {
1287 // Backward bucket redefinition.
1288 define_buckets<Direction::Backward>();
1289 });
1290
1291 // Regenerate arcs after updating bucket splits.
1292 generate_arcs();
1293
1294 // Process jump bucket arcs in parallel.
1295 PARALLEL_SECTIONS(
1296 workB, bi_sched, SECTION { ObtainJumpBucketArcs<Direction::Forward>(); },
1297 SECTION { ObtainJumpBucketArcs<Direction::Backward>(); });
1298
1299 return true;
1300 }
1301 return false;
1302 }
